metadata.description: Consolidated plan to take a raw product idea from concept through launched, monitored software product. ~190 steps (down from 328 in v2) plus a 15-step feature template (down from 31). Designed for AI agent execution with human-in-the-loop via needs_clarification mechanism. Supports both greenfield and existing project onboarding via Phase -1. Every step has difficulty, tools_hint, and artifact_schema annotations.
metadata.clarification_mechanism: Any agent can trigger needs_clarification status. The assistant agent formulates the question, sends via Telegram, interprets the human reply, and resumes the task with clarification context. Reminders are sent every 24 hours if no response. After 72 hours, the orchestrator logs the blocker and pauses the pipeline.
metadata.artifact_convention: Each step reads input_artifacts from prior steps and produces output_artifacts. The orchestrator must pass artifact contents as context to the executing agent. Every artifact name must be globally unique across the entire plan.
metadata.template_convention: Steps with type=template are reusable sequences. They are instantiated by referencing the template_id and providing template_params. The implementation_backlog lists items that each instantiate the feature_implementation_template.

review_policy.escalation_message_template: Review cycle for step '{step_name}' has failed {max_review_cycles} times. Issues: {issues_list}. Human decision needed: fix manually, accept as-is, or provide direction.

revision_policy.description: Allows controlled feedback from later phases back to earlier phase artifacts without full phase re-entry.

allowed_revisions.mechanism: Architecture alignment check at sprint boundaries produces mini-ADRs and updates the relevant artifact sections in-place.

onboarding_policy.description: Controls behavior when the engine operates on an existing codebase rather than a greenfield project.

conditional_groups.description: If fewer than 3 direct competitors exist, run a lighter research path.

fallback_steps.name: competitor_feature_scan_lite
fallback_steps.instruction: Fewer than 3 direct competitors found. Do a lightweight feature scan of whatever direct competitors exist, plus analyze indirect competitors more deeply. For each competitor or indirect alternative: catalog visible features from their website, app store listing, and documentation. Build a combined feature matrix covering both direct and indirect competitors. Note: a thin competitive landscape is itself a signal â€” either blue ocean opportunity or no viable market. Flag this for the go/no-go assessment.
fallback_steps.done_when: competitor_features_raw and competitor_feature_matrix exist with whatever competitive data is available.

conditional_groups.description: Skip realtime design if no functional requirements need it.

fallback_steps.name: realtime_not_needed
fallback_steps.instruction: No realtime requirements identified. Document this decision and recommend the simplest future path to add realtime (e.g., SSE for notifications, WebSocket for collaboration). Output a brief document stating realtime is not needed for MVP with a migration path.
fallback_steps.done_when: realtime_design exists as a brief 'not needed' document with future migration path.

conditional_groups.description: Skip payment-related steps if the product is free.
Skip app store submission if no native mobile apps.
Skip file upload security testing if no upload functionality.
Skip SEO if product has no public web pages.
Skip email launch if no waitlist or email list exists.

phases.name: Existing Project Onboarding
phases.goal: Reverse-engineer an existing project into the plan's artifact format and determine execution starting point.
phases.condition: Only execute if input includes existing_codebase_path

phases.name: Idea Capture & Clarification
phases.goal: Turn a vague idea into a crisp, validated problem statement with clear constraints.

phases.name: Market & Competitive Research
phases.goal: Understand the market landscape, competitors, user needs, and regulatory environment.

phases.name: Product Strategy & Definition
phases.goal: Define what we are building, for whom, why it wins, and what the MVP scope is.

phases.name: Requirements Engineering
phases.goal: Convert product requirements into precise, testable technical requirements.

phases.name: Architecture & Technical Design
phases.goal: Design the complete technical foundation: stack, schema, APIs, infrastructure patterns.

phases.name: UX/UI Design Specification
phases.goal: Fully specify every screen, component, interaction, and responsive behavior.
phases.note: Can run in parallel with phase_4. Does not depend on tech stack decisions.

phases.name: Project Planning & Sprint Setup
phases.goal: Break all work into estimated, ordered, dependency-mapped sprints.

phases.name: Development Environment Setup
phases.goal: Set up repositories, tooling, CI/CD, infrastructure, and design system implementation.

phases.name: Core Implementation
phases.goal: Build every MVP feature through repeatable implementation cycles.

phases.name: Comprehensive Testing
phases.goal: Ensure reliability, performance, accessibility, and cross-platform correctness.

phases.name: Security Hardening
phases.goal: Audit and fix all security vulnerabilities before public exposure.

phases.name: Documentation
phases.goal: Document everything for users, developers, and operators.

phases.name: Legal & Compliance
phases.goal: Ensure legal coverage: ToS, privacy policy, GDPR, license compliance.

phases.name: Pre-Launch Preparation
phases.goal: Production infrastructure, monitoring, analytics, and final QA.

phases.name: Launch
phases.goal: Go live safely and announce to the world.

phases.name: Post-Launch Operations & Iteration
phases.goal: Monitor, stabilize, collect feedback, iterate, and grow.

steps.name: codebase_discovery
steps.instruction: Scan the repository and catalog everything. Use file_tree and index_workspace to get a complete picture. Produce codebase_inventory with sections: repository_info (git history length, branch structure, last commit date, contributor count), tech_stack_detected (languages with file counts, frameworks detected from config files, database type from connection strings or migration files, cache detected, hosting detected from CI/CD configs), project_structure (directory tree with purpose inference for each directory), dependencies (list all from package.json/requirements.txt/go.mod with versions), database_schema_current (from migration files or ORM schema: list all tables, columns, types, indexes), api_endpoints_current (from route files: list all endpoints with method, path, middleware), test_inventory (test file count, framework detected, coverage if measurable from config), ci_cd_detected (pipeline files found, stages defined, deployment targets), configuration_files (list all config files with purpose), documentation_found (README, docs folder, inline comments quality estimate).
steps.done_when: codebase_inventory exists with all sections populated from the existing codebase.

steps.name: code_quality_assessment
steps.instruction: Assess the codebase quality. Run the project's linter if configured, or apply standard linter for the detected language. Check: linting (errors and warnings count), type_safety (is TypeScript strict? are there any-types? is Python typed?), test_coverage (run coverage tool if possible, identify untested critical paths), code_patterns (consistent architecture? service layer separation? error handling pattern?), security_surface (hardcoded secrets scan, dependency vulnerability scan, auth implementation review), technical_debt_visible (TODO/FIXME/HACK comments, disabled tests, commented-out code, dead code). Produce: quality_score (1-10 per category), critical_issues (things that must be fixed before adding features), patterns_to_preserve (good patterns the codebase uses that we should follow).
steps.done_when: code_quality_report exists with per-category scores, critical issues, and patterns to preserve.

steps.name: artifact_synthesis
steps.instruction: For each artifact in the plan, attempt to synthesize it from the existing project. Produce two outputs. synthesized_artifacts: a collection where each entry has artifact_name, content (the synthesized artifact in the plan's expected format), source (where in the codebase this was extracted from), confidence (high: directly extracted from clear source, medium: inferred from multiple signals, low: guessed based on conventions, absent: could not determine). artifact_confidence_map: a flat mapping of artifact_name to confidence level. Artifacts to synthesize: tech_stack_decision (from package.json, configs), database_schema (from migrations/ORM), api_endpoints (from route files), auth_design (from auth middleware and token handling), frontend_architecture (from folder structure and component patterns), design_tokens (from CSS variables, Tailwind config, or theme files), business_rules (from validation logic in code), error_handling_strategy (from error middleware), logging_strategy (from logger config), caching_strategy (from cache usage in code). Do NOT synthesize: product strategy artifacts (vision, personas, USP) or market research artifacts.
steps.done_when: synthesized_artifacts and artifact_confidence_map exist covering all technical artifacts extractable from code.

steps.name: project_state_mapping
steps.instruction: Map the existing project's state onto the plan's step structure. For every step in the plan, determine its status: completed (all output artifacts exist with high confidence AND quality assessment shows no critical issues in that area), partially_completed (artifacts exist but with medium/low confidence, OR quality issues exist that need addressing), needs_execution (output artifacts are absent), not_applicable (the step doesn't apply), needs_human_input (the step produces strategic artifacts that cannot be extracted from code alone). Group results by phase. For each phase: calculate completion_percentage, list blocking_gaps (artifacts that are absent and block downstream phases), list quality_gaps (artifacts that exist but need improvement). Identify the effective_starting_phase: the earliest phase that has incomplete steps which block all downstream work.
steps.done_when: project_state_map exists with per-step status, per-phase completion percentage, and effective starting phase identified.

steps.name: onboarding_strategy
steps.instruction: Based on project_state_map, create the execution plan for this project. Determine execution_mode: greenfield_phases_only, implementation_continuation, hardening_and_launch, post_launch_operations, or selective_improvement. Present to human: project_state_map summary, detected execution_mode, proposed step sequence (which steps to run, which to skip), estimated effort, critical gaps that need human input. Trigger needs_clarification to get human confirmation.
steps.done_when: onboarding_plan exists with execution_mode, step sequence, and human confirmation received.

steps.name: convention_extraction
steps.instruction: Extract coding conventions that all future generated code must follow. Analyze: naming_conventions (camelCase vs snake_case for variables, PascalCase for components, file naming pattern), file_organization (where do new routes go? where do new components go? where do tests go relative to source?), import_style (absolute vs relative, alias usage, import ordering), error_handling_pattern, test_patterns (describe/it vs test, mocking approach, fixture patterns, assertion style), api_patterns (response format, pagination style, authentication attachment, validation approach), component_patterns (functional vs class, hooks usage, prop patterns, styling approach), state_management_patterns, git_conventions (branch naming from git log, commit message format from history). Output a conventions document that will be injected as context to every implementer and coder agent step.
steps.done_when: project_conventions exists with all detected patterns documented for agent consumption.

steps.name: human_strategic_input
steps.instruction: For artifacts that cannot be synthesized from code (product vision, target audience, business model, competitive positioning), collect this information from the human. Format questions based on what's missing. Present synthesized artifacts for human validation. Get human confirmation of the onboarding_plan execution mode and step sequence.
steps.done_when: strategic_context exists with human-provided product context filling gaps code couldn't provide.

steps.name: reverse_pitch_draft
steps.instruction:
Draft the reverse-pitch / Amazon working-backwards artifact (Z1 A1) as a one-page launch press release. Write `mission_{mission_id}/.charter/reverse_pitch.md` with EXACTLY these five top-level sections in this order: `## Headline`, `## Sub-head`, `## Customer Quote`, `## Founder Quote`, `## FAQ`. The FAQ must contain 3-5 questions, each formatted as a `**Q: ...**` line followed by an `A: ...` line. Customer-language only — no marketing jargon. No placeholder text (TODO, TBD, lorem ipsum, <fill in>); the mechanical post-hook verify_reverse_pitch_shape rejects them. Ground the artifact in the founder pitch (mission.description) and the strategic_context input.

Also emit the JSON envelope for schema-version tracking:
```json
{"_schema_version": "1", "path": "mission_{mission_id}/.charter/reverse_pitch.md"}
```
steps.done_when: reverse_pitch.md exists with all required sections (Headline / Sub-head / Customer Quote / Founder Quote / FAQ) and no placeholder text.

steps.name: reverse_pitch_shape_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical post-check that 0.0z produced a paraflow-shape reverse pitch (Z1 A1). No LLM. Failure surfaces missing sections / placeholder text and forces a regenerate.
steps.done_when: verify_reverse_pitch_shape returns ok=true.

steps.name: reverse_pitch_confirm

payload.question: Reverse pitch draft below. Tap a button to confirm, regenerate, or edit.

steps.instruction: Founder confirmation gate for the AI-drafted reverse pitch (Z1 A1). Hands control back to the founder via Telegram keyboard. Reply OK → step completes and 0.0a (intake_todo) runs. Reply 'regenerate' → 0.0z re-fires with the founder's edits as context. Replacing the legacy founder-typed press-release flow per 2026-05-14 feedback.
steps.done_when: founder confirms via Telegram (clarify keyboard returns OK).

steps.name: generate_intake_todo
steps.instruction: Generate the intake todo (Z1 B1) — the ONLY structured gate before charter generation. Replaces the per-question interrogation in legacy 0.4/0.5. Reads founder pitch + Z0 outputs + reverse_pitch.md and emits 10-15 todo items covering audience / problem / outcome / scope / differentiation / constraints / surfaces / brand / risk. Founder confirms via Telegram (status flips back to in_progress); founder edits the file → todo regenerates on next run.
steps.done_when: intake_todo.md exists with 10-15 items AND founder has confirmed via Telegram (clarify-shape).

steps.name: product_charter
steps.instruction:
Write the product charter as a SINGLE coherent document (paraflow shape, Z1 C1+A9+C6+A14). Output `mission_{mission_id}/.charter/product_charter.md` with EXACTLY these five top-level `## ` sections in order:

1. **Product Positioning** — one paragraph naming the audience, the category, and the one-line bet vs. the alternatives.
2. **Brand Keywords** — at least 5 bullets, each `**name** — one-line description`.
3. **Core Problem / JTBD** — one paragraph naming the felt pain and the job-to-be-done.
4. **Goals & Mission** — a `Mission:` line + a `Desired Outcomes` sub-header with 4-7 outcome bullets.
5. **Solutions We Own** — 3 to 7 solutions, each as a `### <Solution Name>` block with ALL of these labeled fields (every solution, no exceptions): `What it solves`, `Typical path`, `Outcome for the user`, `Boundaries`, `Guiding principles`.

The document must read as one mind, not five disjoint LLM passes. The reverse pitch headline should be defensible from the charter; the intake-todo confirmations must be reflected. Do NOT use placeholder text (TODO, TBD, <fill in>, lorem ipsum); the mechanical post-hook verify_charter_shape rejects them.

Also emit a JSON envelope alongside the markdown for schema-version tracking:
```json
{"_schema_version": "1", "path": "mission_{mission_id}/.charter/product_charter.md"}
```
steps.done_when: product_charter.md exists at `mission_{mission_id}/.charter/product_charter.md`, passes verify_charter_shape (5 sections present, 3-7 solutions each with full sub-shape, no placeholders), and is referenced as the mission's charter for downstream phases.

steps.name: product_charter_shape_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical post-check that 0.1 produced a paraflow-shape product charter (Z1 C1+A9+C6+A14). No LLM. Asserts 5 sections + 3-7 solutions each with full sub-shape (boundaries + guiding-principles required) + no placeholder text. Failure forces 0.1 to regenerate.
steps.done_when: verify_charter_shape returns ok=true.

steps.name: problem_statement_extraction
steps.instruction: From the raw_idea_document, extract and articulate the core problem being solved. Output a structured problem statement with these fields: problem_description (1-3 sentences describing the pain), affected_audience (who has this problem), current_alternatives (how they cope today, if inferable), severity (how painful this is, if inferable), frequency (how often they encounter it, if inferable). Mark any field as 'Needs validation' if it cannot be confidently inferred from the raw idea.
steps.done_when: A structured problem_statement exists with all fields populated or explicitly marked as needing validation.

steps.name: assumption_identification
steps.instruction: Identify every assumption embedded in the raw idea. For each assumption, output: assumption_id (A-001, A-002...), statement (the assumption in clear language), category (one of: market, user, technical, business, legal, competitive), risk_if_wrong (what happens if this assumption is false), validation_method (how we could validate this). Common categories to check: 'Users exist who want this', 'Users will pay for this', 'This is technically feasible', 'No legal blockers exist', 'We can differentiate from competitors', 'The market is large enough'.
steps.done_when: A numbered assumption_list exists with at least 5 assumptions identified, each with all fields completed.

steps.name: interview_script_generation
steps.instruction:
Generate `mission_{mission_id}/.intake/interview_script.md` (Z1 A4) — a neutral, non-leading interview script targeting the 5-7 highest-`risk_if_wrong` assumptions from `assumption_list`. Use the product_charter (when present) to identify target personas.

Format the file EXACTLY:

```
---
_schema_version: "1"
mission_id: <id>
target_assumptions: [A-001, A-002, ...]
target_personas: [<persona names>]
question_count: <5..7>
---

# Interview Script

## Logistics
- Length: 25-35 min
- Format: 1:1 video; record with consent
- Sample size minimum: 3 per persona

## Questions

### Q1 — <short topic for the assumption being probed>
**Question:** <neutral wording — never leading, never suggesting an answer>
**Probes:**
- <follow-up>
- <follow-up>
**Looking for:** <what answer pattern would FALSIFY the assumption>

### Q2 — ...
```

Question-writing rules:
1. Never lead the witness — no "don't you think...", no "wouldn't it be great if...".
2. Each question targets ONE assumption (cite the assumption_id in the topic).
3. "Looking for" must describe the FALSIFYING answer pattern, not the confirming one — interview value is in disconfirmation.
4. 5-7 questions total. More than 7 burns attention budget; fewer than 5 isn't a real script.

Mechanical post-hook `0.0c.verify` will reject the file if the front matter is malformed, the question count is out of range, or any question is missing Question/Probes/Looking-for fields.
steps.done_when: interview_script.md exists with valid front matter, Logistics section, and 5-7 well-formed Q blocks. Verified by 0.0c.verify.

steps.name: interview_script_shape_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical post-check that 0.0c produced a well-formed interview script (Z1 A4). No LLM. Asserts front matter (_schema_version=1, mission_id, target_assumptions non-empty), Logistics section present, 5-7 Q blocks each with Question/Probes (with bullets)/Looking-for fields, no placeholder text. Failure forces 0.0c to regenerate.
steps.done_when: verify_interview_script_shape returns ok=true.

steps.name: request_interview_data
steps.instruction: Surface the generated interview script to the founder via Telegram (Z1 A4). Founder replies DONE (after dropping transcripts into mission_{mission_id}/.intake/interviews/) or SKIP (mission proceeds at own risk; reviewer 1.13 grades by ambition tier — soft warn at private_beta+, hard reject at public_launch).
steps.done_when: Founder has acknowledged via DONE or SKIP.

steps.name: scope_ambiguity_detection
steps.instruction: Review all input artifacts. Identify every ambiguity, undefined term, or scope question that must be resolved before proceeding. Generate a numbered list of open questions organized by category: Audience (who exactly?), Scope (what's in/out?), Business Model (how does this make money?), Constraints (budget, timeline, tech, team?), Platform (web, mobile, desktop?), Differentiation (why us over competitors?), Priority (what matters most: speed, quality, features, cost?). Each question should be specific enough that a yes/no or short answer resolves it.
steps.done_when: An open_questions_list exists with all identified ambiguities as clear, answerable questions.

steps.name: compliance_fingerprint_collection
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 5A (P6) — collect compliance fingerprint. Reads `mission_<id>/.preflight/compliance_fingerprint.json` from z0 when present and writes the merged fingerprint to `mission_<id>/compliance_fingerprint.json`. Telegram clarify deltas are fired only for fields the founder did not pre-declare at z0.
steps.done_when: compliance_fingerprint.json exists with _schema_version, jurisdictions, user_classes, data_categories_coarse fields populated (or empty arrays at prototype tier).

steps.name: human_clarification_request
steps.instruction: Review the open_questions_list and assumption_list. Pick only the TOP 5 most impactful questions â€” the ones whose answers most change the project direction. Keep each question short (1-2 sentences max). For each, suggest 2-3 concrete answer options the human can pick from (e.g. 'A) Families B) Roommates C) Both'). Do NOT ask more than 5 questions â€” if in doubt, cut the less important ones. You MUST use the clarify action to send these questions to the human.
steps.done_when: clarification_request has been sent to the human via the clarify action.

steps.name: idea_brief_compilation_and_review
steps.instruction: Compile inputs into a clean Idea Brief document with these sections: 1) Vision (one paragraph), 2) Problem Statement, 3) Target Audience, 4) Proposed Solution, 5) Key Constraints, 6) Initial Scope Boundaries, 7) Assumptions, 8) Open Risks. PREFERENCE ORDER for source data: when `product_charter` is present (modern Z1 missions), distill its Positioning + JTBD + Solutions into the brief and treat the charter as canonical. When the charter is absent (legacy missions), fall back to `problem_statement` + `clarification_answers` (the old phase-0 micro-artifacts). Write in clear, professional prose. Self-review: check for internal contradictions, anything from charter or clarifications not reflected, vague language. Output the final versioned document as idea_brief_final with a version number and date.
steps.done_when: idea_brief_final exists as a clean, self-reviewed, internally consistent document with all 8 sections.

steps.name: non_goals_lock

payload.question:
List 3-7 mission-wide non-goals — explicit refusals that downstream phases must respect. Each one-line. Examples: 'No real-time multiplayer features.' / 'No mobile-app version (web-only at launch).' / 'No support for under-13 users.' / 'No vendor onboarding workflow this iteration.' Charter has per-solution boundaries; this is the higher-level cross-mission refusal set. Reply with markdown matching: `---
_schema_version: "1"
mission_id: <id>
non_goals:
  - "..."
---

# Non-goals

- ...`

steps.instruction: Mission-wide non-goals lock (Z1 A2). Founder authors OR confirms an analyst-suggested list of 3-7 explicit refusals. Output is `mission_{mission_id}/.charter/non_goals.md` with YAML frontmatter (`_schema_version: "1"` + `non_goals:` array) and a `# Non-goals` body section mirroring the bullets. The charter (0.1) carries per-solution `boundaries`; this artifact captures the cross-mission refusals at the higher level. Reviewers at 3.11 / 4.16 / 5.10 reject any FR / architectural choice / feature design that maps to these non-goals.
steps.done_when: non_goals.md exists with YAML frontmatter (_schema_version=1, non_goals array of 3-7 entries), '# Non-goals' heading, and 3-7 body bullets. No placeholder text.

steps.name: non_goals_shape_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical verifier (Z1 A2): asserts non_goals.md has YAML frontmatter with _schema_version='1', a non_goals array (3-7), '# Non-goals' body heading, 3-7 body bullets, no placeholders.
steps.done_when: verify_non_goals_shape returns ok=true.

steps.name: prior_art_search
steps.instruction: Call find_prior_art(idea_summary=idea_brief_final.summary, domain_keywords=<comma-separated tags from idea_brief_final.domain_tags or Vision keywords>, k=10, ambition_tier=context.ambition_tier or 'private_beta'). Inspect the returned report. If verdict='blue_ocean_validated' but you suspect the agent missed adjacent solutions, broaden domain_keywords once and re-call. Save the final report JSON to the produces path verbatim. Surface dead/dormant entries with Wayback timestamps when present.
steps.done_when: prior_art_report exists at the produces path and prior_art_min_coverage post-hook returns ok=true.

steps.name: market_existence_research
steps.instruction:
Using the idea_brief_final, research whether a market exists for this problem. Search for: market size estimates (TAM, SAM, SOM if available), market growth rate, industry reports or analyst coverage, investment activity in this space (recent funding rounds), Google Trends data for related keywords, job postings in this space (indicates industry activity). Use web search tools. Check industry-specific sources. Output structured findings: market_size (with source), growth_trend (with source), investment_signals (list of recent fundings), search_interest_trend (direction), industry_reports (list with links), confidence_level (high/medium/low with justification).

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: market_existence_data exists with sourced data points covering market size, growth, and investment activity.

steps.name: target_audience_research
steps.instruction:
Research the target audience defined in idea_brief_final. Find: demographics (age, location, occupation, income level), psychographics (values, attitudes, lifestyle), online behavior (which platforms, subreddits, forums, communities they use), pain points (what they complain about related to this problem), current tools/solutions they use, willingness to pay signals (do they pay for similar tools?), technology adoption level. Search Reddit, Twitter/X, forums, Quora, Stack Overflow, Product Hunt discussions, industry surveys. Output structured findings per category with source links.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: audience_research_data exists with demographic, psychographic, behavioral, and pain point data with sources.

steps.name: direct_competitor_identification
steps.instruction:
Identify all direct competitors: products solving the same problem for the same audience. For each competitor, collect: name, website_url, one_line_description, founding_year, funding_total (if known), team_size_estimate, platforms (web/ios/android/desktop), primary_market (geography), status (active/acquired/dead). Search: web search, Product Hunt, G2, Capterra, AlternativeTo, Crunchbase, App Store, Play Store. Find at least 5 competitors if they exist. If fewer than 3 exist, note this as a market signal (either blue ocean or no market).

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: direct_competitors_list exists as a structured list with all fields populated per competitor.

steps.name: indirect_competitor_identification
steps.instruction:
Identify indirect competitors: products that solve the same problem differently, or solve an adjacent problem for the same audience. For each, collect: name, website_url, how_they_relate, threat_level (high/medium/low). Also identify potential future competitors: large companies that could enter this space easily.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep each competitor entry under 200 words. Focus on name, URL, relation, and threat level.
steps.done_when: indirect_competitors_list exists with categorized indirect competitors and future threat assessment.

steps.name: competitive_positioning_lock
steps.instruction:
Write `mission_{mission_id}/.prd/competitive_positioning.md` (Z1 C2) — the paraflow PRD §6 named-competitor positioning section. Synthesize from `direct_competitors_list` (1.3), `indirect_competitors_list` (1.4) and the `product_charter`.

The file MUST follow this exact shape:

```
---
_schema_version: "1"
mission_id: <id>
named_competitors: [<name1>, <name2>, ...]
---

## Landscape
<one paragraph naming each competitor + the audience they serve>

## Value Thesis
<each named player's value proposition; one bullet or paragraph per player>

## Strengths / Weaknesses
<experience pros/cons per player>

## Our Differentiators
<our unique points; tie back to charter brand-keywords + Solutions We Own. Cite the charter solution names (or solution IDs if numbered) you are leveraging — the reviewer at 1.13 rejects if differentiators do not cite charter solutions.>

## Switching Costs & Risks
<migration costs and adoption risks for users coming from the named players>

## Notes
<reference links / inspiration patterns>
```

Rules:
1. `named_competitors` must be non-empty — pull names from the direct + indirect lists.
2. Every player named in `named_competitors` must show up in Landscape, Value Thesis, and Strengths/Weaknesses.
3. Differentiators must cite at least one charter Solution name verbatim (or by labeled ID).
4. Switching Costs & Risks must be a real paragraph — single-line stub fails verify.
5. No placeholder text (TODO, TBD, <fill in>, lorem ipsum).

Mechanical post-hook `1.4a.verify` will reject malformed shape, missing sections, empty `named_competitors`, or placeholder text.
steps.done_when: competitive_positioning.md exists at mission_{mission_id}/.prd/ with all six sections, non-empty named_competitors, and passes verify_competitive_positioning_shape.

steps.name: competitive_positioning_shape_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical post-check that 1.4a produced a paraflow-PRD-§6-shape positioning doc (Z1 C2). No LLM. Asserts front matter (_schema_version=1, mission_id, named_competitors non-empty), six required sections present and non-empty, no placeholders. Failure forces 1.4a to regenerate.
steps.done_when: verify_competitive_positioning_shape returns ok=true.

steps.name: competitor_feature_analysis
steps.instruction:
For each direct competitor in direct_competitors_list, catalog every feature visible from their: marketing website (features page, pricing page), documentation, free tier exploration, App Store/Play Store listing, Product Hunt page, demo videos. For each feature, record: feature_name, feature_description, which_competitor, tier_available (free/paid/enterprise), and category (core, secondary, integration, admin). Be exhaustive. Then build a feature comparison matrix. Rows: every unique feature (deduplicated and normalized across competitors). Columns: each competitor. Cell values: 'full', 'partial', 'none', 'unknown'. Group rows by feature category. Add a 'feature_frequency' column. Sort within each category by frequency descending.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep the feature matrix concise  -  max 10 features per competitor, one line each. Total output under 2000 words.
steps.done_when: competitor_features_raw and competitor_feature_matrix exist with comprehensive feature data and comparison table.

steps.name: competitor_pricing_analysis
steps.instruction:
For each direct competitor, document their pricing model completely: pricing_model_type, tiers (each with name, monthly_price, annual_price, key_limits), free_tier_scope, upgrade_triggers, enterprise_offering, pricing_page_url. Then analyze: market standard pricing range, most common model, pricing gaps, free-to-paid feature gates.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep pricing analysis concise  -  one paragraph per competitor. Total under 1000 words.
steps.done_when: competitor_pricing_analysis exists with per-competitor pricing details and cross-market pricing insights.

steps.name: competitor_review_and_sentiment
steps.instruction:
For each major competitor (top 5), collect user reviews and sentiment from: App Store, Play Store, G2, Capterra, TrustRadius, Product Hunt comments, Reddit mentions, Twitter/X mentions, Hacker News discussions. For each source: source_name, review_count_sampled, average_rating, positive_themes, negative_themes, notable_quotes (3-5 per competitor). Focus on recent reviews (last 12 months). Then synthesize sentiment. Per competitor: top_5_praised_aspects, top_5_pain_points, overall_sentiment, net_promoter_signal. Cross-competitor: universal_praises (table stakes), universal_complaints (opportunities), differentiation_signals.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep sentiment concise  -  max 5 bullet points per competitor for positives and negatives. Total under 1500 words.
steps.done_when: competitor_reviews_raw and competitor_sentiment_analysis exist with per-competitor and cross-competitor sentiment synthesis.

steps.name: competitor_ux_analysis
steps.instruction:
For each major competitor (top 5), evaluate their UX based on publicly available information: website structure, app store screenshots descriptions, user reviews mentioning UX, documentation quality. Do NOT try to analyze images directly â€” instead, assess UX from text descriptions, review comments about UI/UX, app store listing descriptions, and website copy. Evaluate each competitor for: design_quality (1-10), visual_consistency, information_density, navigation_clarity, onboarding_quality, accessibility_signals, mobile_quality, unique_ux_patterns, ux_weaknesses. Output per competitor with overall_ux_rank.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep UX evaluation concise  -  max 3 strengths and 3 weaknesses per competitor. Total under 1000 words.
steps.done_when: competitor_ux_evaluation exists with per-competitor UX assessment and comparative ranking.

steps.name: market_gap_analysis
steps.instruction:
Cross-reference features users request but no competitor provides, features where all competitors are 'partial', sentiment weaknesses common across competitors, pricing gaps. Also produce a SWOT analysis for each major competitor (top 3-5) with specific, evidence-based statements. Output a ranked list of market opportunities: opportunity_id, description, evidence, estimated_impact, difficulty_to_address, our_ability_to_exploit. Rank by impact x feasibility. Wire indirect_competitors_list into analysis to catch substitute threats.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: market_gap_analysis exists with a ranked, evidence-based list of market opportunities and per-competitor SWOT.

steps.name: technology_trend_research
steps.instruction:
Research technology trends relevant to the product domain. Find: new APIs or services, AI/ML capabilities, emerging platforms, open-source tools that reduce build cost. For each: name, description, relevance_to_our_product, maturity_level (experimental/emerging/mature), adoption_risk.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: technology_trends document exists with relevant tech trends and their applicability assessed.

steps.name: regulatory_research
steps.instruction:
Identify legal, regulatory, and compliance requirements for this product. Check: data privacy (GDPR, CCPA), industry-specific regulation (HIPAA, PCI-DSS, COPPA, FINRA), platform policies, accessibility law, intellectual property concerns, open-source license obligations. For each: requirement_name, applies_if, summary_of_obligations, compliance_effort, consequence_of_non_compliance.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: regulatory_requirements exists with all applicable regulations identified and obligations summarized.

steps.name: compliance_overlay
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 5A (P6) — for each <jurisdiction × data_category> combination in compliance_fingerprint, identify required_documents (privacy_policy, cookie_banner, dpa, tos, retention_policy, age_gate, accessibility_statement, data_processing_record). Call `compliance_template_render(fingerprint, doc_type, lang)` for each. Save rendered output to `generated_template_path` under `mission_<id>/.compliance/legal/<doc>.md`. Surface cross_jurisdiction_conflicts when present. Set requires_legal_review=true on every doc; founder_review_required=true on privacy_policy, dpa, tos. Emit the `compliance_overlay` JSON to `mission_<id>/compliance_overlay.json`.
steps.done_when: compliance_overlay.required_documents non-empty (or fingerprint declares jurisdictions=[]) and every generated_template_path file exists.

steps.name: market_research_synthesis
steps.instruction: Synthesize market and competitor research into a Research Summary under 500 words. Sections: Market Viability, Target Audience Profile, Competitive Landscape, Key Risks.
steps.done_when: research_summary exists as a well-structured summary document.

steps.name: market_research_report
steps.instruction: Using research_summary plus gap analysis, technology trends, regulatory data, and indirect competitors, compile the full Market Research Report. Sections: Executive Summary, Market Overview, Competitive Landscape, Market Gap Analysis, Recommendations.
steps.done_when: market_research_report exists as a comprehensive, well-structured document.

steps.name: research_quality_review
steps.instruction:
Review market_research_report AND (when present, modern Z1 missions) product_charter + reverse_pitch + competitive_positioning + interview_script for:

Research quality:
1) Claims without evidence,
2) Logical gaps,
3) Missing data,
4) Bias,
5) Internal contradictions,
6) Feature matrix and pricing completeness,
7) Gap analysis quality.

Charter coherence (Z1 Tier 1 — only when product_charter is present):
8) Charter coherence — do Positioning, Brand Keywords, JTBD, Mission, and Solutions read as one mind, or as 5 disjoint LLM passes? Flag inconsistencies.
9) Solutions completeness — every Solution must carry a populated `Boundaries` field AND a `Guiding principles` field. Reject if any solution lacks either, or if the section uses placeholder text.
10) Reverse-pitch alignment — does the charter support the press-release headline, sub-head, customer quote, and founder quote? Flag any contradiction (e.g. headline promises 'free forever' but charter has a paid tier).

Competitive positioning (Z1 Tier 2 C2 — only when competitive_positioning is present):
11) competitive_positioning.md exists with all six sections (Landscape / Value Thesis / Strengths-Weaknesses / Our Differentiators / Switching Costs & Risks / Notes) and `named_competitors` is non-empty.
12) Differentiators cite at least one Solution from the charter by name (or labeled ID). Reject if Differentiators are generic and do not reference any charter Solution.
13) Switching Costs & Risks section is non-empty (real prose, not a stub).

Interview-script grounding (Z1 Tier 2 A4 — only when interview_script is present):
14) interview_script.md exists. If `interview_count == 0` (no transcripts in mission_{mission_id}/.intake/interviews/) AND `mission.ambition` is `private_beta` or higher, soft-warn (verdict=needs_minor_fixes with issue tagged 14). If `mission.ambition` is `public_launch`, hard-reject (verdict=fail).

Prior-art coverage (Z1 Tier 6 B / P5 — only when prior_art_report is present):
15) prior_art_report.attempted_solutions must be non-empty AND have at least 2 entries with a resolvable URL (status != 'dead'). If 0-1 resolvable entries AND `mission.ambition` ∈ {private_beta, public_launch, revenue_product}, soft-warn (verdict=needs_minor_fixes).
16) key_lessons must be non-empty — generic platitudes ('failed due to bad UX') without a concrete signal (revenue, churn, launch year, founder quote) count as missing. Reject if all lessons are platitudes.
17) verdict field on the report must NOT be 'inconclusive' when graveyard_count >= 3. Three or more dead/dormant prior attempts is a strong negative signal that the founder must acknowledge.

Reject (verdict != pass) if any section uses placeholder text (TODO, TBD, <fill in>, lorem ipsum) or if any solution lacks `boundaries` / `guiding_principles` or if checks 11-13 fail or if check 16 fails. Output: verdict (pass/fail/needs_minor_fixes), issues list — each issue tagged by which check (1-17) it failed.

IMPORTANT: produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching — use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: research_review_result exists with clear quality assessment.

steps.name: go_no_go_assessment
steps.instruction:
Evaluate all research to produce a Go/No-Go recommendation. Score 1-10: market_attractiveness, competitive_feasibility, technical_feasibility, differentiation_potential, regulatory_risk. Compute weighted_score. Wire indirect_competitors_list into the feasibility assessment. If score > 6: Go. If 4-6: Conditional â€” trigger needs_clarification. If < 4: No-Go.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.

Z1 P5 — If `prior_art_report.verdict == 'graveyard_well_populated'`, weight the kill-decision higher; surface `prior_art_report.key_lessons` as additional risks in your reasoning and cite at least one lesson_id explicitly.
steps.done_when: go_no_go_decision exists with scored assessment and clear recommendation.

steps.name: product_vision_and_positioning
steps.instruction:
Craft a product vision statement: 'For [target audience] who [need], [product name] is a [category] that [key benefit]. Unlike [competitors], we [key differentiator].' Also write a tagline under 10 words. Then craft a product mission statement: 'We help [specific audience] [achieve specific outcome] by [how the product does it].' Under 30 words. Self-review for alignment with go_no_go_decision and market_research_report.

Z1 P5 — Prior art report's `key_lessons` are positioning constraints — explicitly reference at least one lesson_id in the positioning rationale (justify how this product's differentiator avoids the cited failure mode).
steps.done_when: product_vision and product_mission exist with vision statement, tagline, and mission.

steps.name: value_proposition_canvas
steps.instruction: Create a Value Proposition Canvas with Customer Profile (jobs, pains, gains) and Value Map (products_and_services, pain_relievers, gain_creators). Draw explicit connections between each pain_reliever and customer_pain, each gain_creator and customer_gain. Identify top 3 pain-gain fits.
steps.done_when: value_proposition_canvas exists with both sides populated and explicit pain-gain connections identified.

steps.name: unique_selling_proposition
steps.instruction: Define 1-3 USPs that are: Valuable, Unique, Credible, Communicable. Reference specific competitor weaknesses and market gaps. If multiple positioning strategies are viable, present all with trade-offs and trigger needs_clarification.
steps.done_when: unique_selling_proposition exists with 1-3 validated USPs.

steps.name: user_personas_and_journeys
steps.instruction:
Create 2-4 distinct user personas with: name, age, occupation, location, tech_savviness, goals, frustrations, current_tools, behavioral_patterns, quote, primary_pain. One persona should be the 'primary' persona. For each persona write 2-3 usage scenarios covering first-time use, regular use, and edge case/advanced use. Then map end-to-end user journey for primary persona through: awareness, consideration, signup, onboarding, first_value, engagement, mastery, expansion, advocacy. For each stage: user_actions, touchpoints, emotions, drop_off_reasons, metrics. Identify the 'aha moment'.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep each persona under 300 words. Max 3 personas. Total under 1500 words.
steps.done_when: user_personas and user_journey_map exist with detailed personas, scenarios, and journey stages.

steps.name: feature_brainstorm
steps.instruction: Generate an exhaustive list of every possible feature from: competitor matrix (table stakes), value proposition canvas (pain relievers, gain creators), user scenarios (product actions), technology trends (tech-enabled features), USP implications. No filtering. For each: feature_id (F-001...), feature_name, description, source, category. Expect 50-200 features.
steps.done_when: feature_brainstorm_list exists with an exhaustive, categorized feature list.

steps.name: feature_prioritization
steps.instruction: Categorize every feature using MoSCoW: Must-have, Should-have, Could-have, Won't-have. Justify each. Must-haves should include: core auth, minimum features to deliver primary value proposition, regulatory requirements. Aim for 15-25 Must-haves, 10-20 Should-haves.
steps.done_when: feature_prioritization exists with every feature categorized and justified.

steps.name: mvp_scope_definition
steps.instruction: Define the MVP: smallest feature set that delivers core value proposition and validates primary USP. Include all Must-haves. Selectively include Should-haves for coherent UX. Output: mvp_feature_list, excluded_features_rationale, estimated_scope_size, mvp_value_statement. If scope >30 or <8 features, trigger needs_clarification.
steps.done_when: mvp_scope exists with a clear feature list, rationale, and scope size estimate.

steps.name: user_stories
steps.instruction:
Write user stories for the MVP features. Each story object MUST have EXACTLY these fields: story_id (US-001, US-002...), epic (short group name), title (short story name), story ('As a [persona], I want [action], so that [benefit]'), acceptance_criteria (Given/When/Then text, testable), priority (High|Medium|Low).

OUTPUT FORMAT: Return ONLY a JSON array starting with `[` and ending with `]`. No wrapping object, no prose, no use cases, no extra fields. 8-15 stories total, each under 80 words.

DO NOT produce use cases, flows, or any fields beyond the six listed above.
steps.done_when: user_stories is a JSON array with 8-15 items, each carrying story_id/epic/title/story/acceptance_criteria/priority.

steps.name: success_metrics_definition
steps.instruction:
Define KPIs using AARRR: Acquisition, Activation, Retention, Revenue, Referral. For each: name, formula, data_source, target_value, measurement_frequency. Define north_star_metric with justification.

Z9 T5B — also classify the product's business_model as one of 'b2b' | 'b2c' | 'hybrid' (default 'b2c' if genuinely ambiguous). B2B north-stars tilt toward MRR / churn / seats; B2C toward activation / retention / referral — let this shape the AARRR target choices. Z9 T5A — also declare target_segment ('paid_users' | 'new_signups' | 'week2_churners' | 'any'): WHO this product's growth metrics primarily track. Emit both as success_metrics fields so they flow into mission.context.
steps.done_when: success_metrics exists with AARRR metrics, targets, measurement methods, a north star metric, business_model, and target_segment identified.

steps.name: monetization_strategy
steps.instruction: Define business model: pricing_model, tiers, free_tier_strategy, upgrade_triggers, billing_implementation, revenue_projections. If multiple viable models exist, present with trade-offs and trigger needs_clarification.
steps.done_when: monetization_strategy exists with pricing tiers, free/paid split, and revenue projections.

steps.name: prd_strategy_section
steps.instruction: Compile the strategy sections of the Product Requirements Document: Vision & Mission, Value Proposition, Unique Selling Proposition, Monetization Strategy. Write as a cohesive document, not just a list of artifacts.
steps.done_when: prd_strategy_section exists as a cohesive strategy document.

steps.name: prd_final
steps.instruction: Merge prd_strategy_section with user personas, MVP scope, user stories, journey map, and success metrics into the final Product Requirements Document. Self-review for contradictions, vague requirements, and missing sections. Add executive summary.
steps.done_when: prd_final exists as a comprehensive, self-reviewed, versioned PRD with all sections.

steps.name: functional_requirements_extraction
steps.instruction:
Extract functional requirements from prd_final. Each requirement object MUST have EXACTLY these fields: req_id (FR-001, FR-002...), title (short name), description (one-paragraph spec, under 80 words), source_story_ids (array of story id strings), priority (High|Medium|Low), category (auth|data|ui|api|integration|notification|search|admin|billing).

Z1 Tier 2 (P4) — falsification triple REQUIRED on every item:
- risk_if_wrong: one of "low" | "medium" | "high" | "critical" — what's the cost if this requirement is wrong?
- validation_method: how would we know it's wrong? Concrete check (e.g., "user interview with N>=3 reseller archetype users", "monitor latency p95 < 500ms over 7d", "fact-check against TR e-commerce regulation"). When risk_if_wrong=critical, validation_method MUST name a measurable / specific check (numbers OR keywords like interview / monitor / test / audit / benchmark).
- falsification_signal: specific observable that would cause us to revisit this requirement.

OUTPUT FORMAT: Return ONLY a JSON array starting with `[` and ending with `]`. No wrapping object, no prose, no extra fields. 8-20 requirements total. Every user story must map to at least one requirement.
steps.done_when: functional_requirements is a JSON array with 8-20 items, each carrying req_id/title/description/source_story_ids/priority/category PLUS risk_if_wrong, validation_method, falsification_signal (Z1 P4 triple).

steps.name: functional_requirements_falsification_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical verifier (Z1 P4): asserts every functional_requirement carries risk_if_wrong + validation_method + falsification_signal, and that risk_if_wrong=critical items name a measurable validation_method.
steps.done_when: verify_falsification_present returns ok=true.

steps.name: nfr_performance_and_scalability
steps.instruction:
Define performance NFRs: api_response_time (p50, p95, p99), page_load_time, time_to_interactive, LCP, FID, CLS, throughput, database_query_time. Adjust targets based on product type and audience. Then define scalability NFRs: concurrent_users, data_volume, file_storage, api_request_volume, background_job_volume, notification_volume. Include current, 6-month, and 12-month projections with 10x burst headroom.

Z1 Tier 2 (P4) — wrap each top-level NFR object with an `items` array where each entry carries: name, target, AND the falsification triple (risk_if_wrong [low|medium|high|critical], validation_method, falsification_signal). When risk_if_wrong=critical, validation_method MUST be measurable (e.g., 'monitor p95 < 500ms over 7d') not vague ('users will tell us').
steps.done_when: nfr_performance and nfr_scalability exist with measurable targets AND each item carries the falsification triple (Z1 P4).

steps.name: nfr_perf_scale_falsification_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical verifier (Z1 P4): asserts every NFR item under nfr_performance.items and nfr_scalability.items carries the falsification triple.
steps.done_when: verify_falsification_present returns ok=true.

steps.name: nfr_availability_and_security
steps.instruction:
Define availability NFRs: uptime_target, maintenance_windows, maximum_unplanned_downtime, RTO, RPO, degraded_mode_behavior per dependency. Then define security requirements covering: authentication, authorization, data_protection, api_security, infrastructure, audit, compliance. Map each regulatory_requirement to specific technical controls.

Z1 Tier 2 (P4) — both artifacts must include an `items` array where each entry has: name, target, AND the falsification triple (risk_if_wrong [low|medium|high|critical], validation_method, falsification_signal). Security items where risk_if_wrong=critical (e.g., authentication, data_protection) MUST name a measurable validation_method (e.g., 'pen-test against OWASP top-10 quarterly').
steps.done_when: nfr_availability and security_requirements exist with measurable targets, traceable security controls, AND every item carries the falsification triple (Z1 P4).

steps.name: nfr_availability_security_falsification_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical verifier (Z1 P4): asserts every nfr_availability.items and security_requirements.items entry carries the falsification triple.
steps.done_when: verify_falsification_present returns ok=true.

steps.name: data_requirements
steps.instruction: Define data requirements: for each entity: entity_name, description, data_fields, sensitivity_level, volume_estimate, retention_policy, deletion_requirements, backup_requirements. Also: data_flows, data_transformations, data_access_patterns, data_archival_strategy.
steps.done_when: data_requirements exists with all data entities, sensitivity, volume, retention, and access patterns defined.

steps.name: integration_requirements
steps.instruction:
Identify all third-party integrations needed. For each: integration_id, service_name, purpose, api_type, authentication_method, rate_limits, pricing, data_exchanged, latency_expectation, failure_mode, alternatives.

IMPORTANT: After using tools (max 2-3 calls), produce your answer as structured text or JSON in your final response. Your response IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: integration_requirements exists with every third-party service identified, evaluated, and failure modes documented.

steps.name: platform_and_accessibility_requirements
steps.instruction:
Define platform support: primary_platform, browser_support, mobile_support, screen_sizes, os_versions, device_types. If multiple platforms equally important, trigger needs_clarification. Define accessibility requirements: target WCAG 2.1 AA minimum. Organized by WCAG principle: perceivable, operable, understandable, robust. Each with req_id, WCAG criterion reference, implementation guidance, testing method. Define i18n requirements: initial_languages, future_languages, rtl_support, date/number/currency/timezone handling, text_expansion_budget, string_extraction_strategy.

IMPORTANT: After using tools (max 2-3 calls), produce your answer as structured text or JSON in your final response. Your response IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: platform_requirements, accessibility_requirements, and i18n_requirements exist.

steps.name: business_rules_extraction
steps.instruction:
Extract every business rule: rule_id (BR-001...), title, rule_statement ('IF condition THEN consequence'), category, source, affected_features, testable_assertion.

Z1 Tier 2 (P4) — falsification triple REQUIRED on every BR:
- risk_if_wrong: one of "low" | "medium" | "high" | "critical".
- validation_method: how would we know this rule is wrong? (e.g., 'fact-check against TR e-commerce regulation', 'A/B against control cohort over 14d').
- falsification_signal: specific observable that would cause us to revisit the rule.

When risk_if_wrong=critical, validation_method MUST name a measurable / specific check.
steps.done_when: business_rules exists as a numbered list of precise, testable business rule statements, each carrying the Z1 P4 falsification triple.

steps.name: business_rules_falsification_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical verifier (Z1 P4): asserts every business_rule carries risk_if_wrong + validation_method + falsification_signal, with measurable validation when risk_if_wrong=critical.
steps.done_when: verify_falsification_present returns ok=true.

steps.name: acceptance_criteria_audit_and_fixes
steps.instruction: Audit all user story acceptance criteria: has Given/When/Then, happy path covered, error/edge case covered, specific not vague, automatable, business rules reflected, no ambiguous terms. Flag any FR or BR with no corresponding acceptance criteria. For every failed story, rewrite/add acceptance criteria to address issues. Use Given/When/Then format. Ensure specific, testable, complete. Output complete updated user_stories_refined (all stories).
steps.done_when: user_stories_refined exists with all acceptance criteria audited and issues resolved.

steps.name: requirements_traceability
steps.instruction: Build traceability matrix: map every functional requirement to user stories and business rules. Identify any requirement without a user story or business rule.
steps.done_when: traceability_matrix exists with all requirements cross-referenced and orphans flagged.

steps.name: requirements_conflict_check
steps.instruction: Check for conflicts between functional requirements and NFRs. Identify where performance targets conflict with feature scope, where security constraints limit features, where scalability needs affect architecture. Document each conflict with resolution.
steps.done_when: conflict_resolutions exists with all conflicts identified and resolved.

steps.name: requirements_spec_functional
steps.instruction: Compile Part 1 of Requirements Specification: Functional Requirements, User Stories, Business Rules, Data Requirements. Format as structured document.
steps.done_when: requirements_spec_part1 exists as a structured document covering functional requirements.

steps.name: requirements_spec_nonfunctional
steps.instruction:
Merge requirements_spec_part1 with non-functional requirements (performance, availability, security) and the traceability matrix into the final Requirements Specification Document.

OUTPUT FORMAT: Return ONLY the Markdown content directly in `result` (no JSON wrapping, no `final_answer` envelope, no `Wrote ...` summary). The Markdown MUST contain these top-level sections in order, each as a `##` heading:

## Functional Requirements
Carry forward functional requirement summaries from requirements_spec_part1.

## Non-Functional Requirements
Performance + availability + scalability bullets from nfr_performance and nfr_availability.

## Security Requirements
Security controls from security_requirements.

## User Stories
List user-story IDs covered by these requirements.

## Traceability Matrix
Table mapping each requirement id to user-story ids and back.
steps.done_when: requirements_spec is a Markdown document with `## Functional Requirements`, `## Non-Functional Requirements`, `## Security Requirements`, `## User Stories`, `## Traceability Matrix` headings present.

steps.name: requirements_review
steps.instruction:
Review requirements_spec against prd_final for completeness, consistency, testability, and feasibility. Check that every user story has traceable requirements, no orphaned requirements exist, NFR targets are realistic, and security requirements cover all sensitive areas.

Z1 Tier 2 (P4) — falsification rigor:
- REJECT (status=fail) if any FR / NFR / BR / system_quality_attribute / persona is missing the falsification triple (risk_if_wrong, validation_method, falsification_signal).
- REJECT when risk_if_wrong='critical' and validation_method is vague (e.g., 'users will tell us', 'we'll see how it goes'). Critical-risk items MUST name a measurable / specific check (numbers OR keywords like interview / monitor / test / audit / benchmark / fact-check).
- REJECT items where falsification_signal is non-observable (e.g., 'something feels off' is not observable; 'p95 latency exceeds 800ms over 3 days' is observable).

Z1 Tier 2 (A2) — non-goals contradiction check:
- The mechanical sibling check_against_non_goals surfaces token-overlap matches between non_goals.md and this artifact body. CONSUME those matches as input — they pre-flag obvious contradictions.
- REJECT (status=fail) any FR / NFR / BR that maps to a non-goal listed in mission_{mission_id}/.charter/non_goals.md (e.g., non-goal='No real-time multiplayer features' → reject any FR introducing WebSocket multiplayer).
- Subtle contradictions are your judgment; the mechanical check only flags lexical overlap.

OUTPUT FORMAT: Return ONLY a JSON object with EXACTLY two top-level fields:
{
  "status": "pass" | "fail" | "needs_revision",
  "issues": [ { "severity": "...", "file": "...", "line": <int|null>, "description": "...", "suggested_fix": "..." }, ... ]
}

IMPORTANT: emit `status` FIRST so it lands even when output is truncated. Cap issues at 12 entries — group similar findings into one issue rather than listing each occurrence.

steps.name: api_resource_model

Define API-wide conventions before any endpoints are designed. These conventions apply to every endpoint in step 4.5b openapi_spec.

OUTPUT FORMAT: Return ONLY the Markdown content directly in `result` (no JSON wrapping, no `final_answer` envelope, no `Wrote ...` summary). The Markdown MUST contain these top-level sections in order, each as a `##` heading:

## Resources
List top-level resources, sub-resources, key relationships. Include base URL.

## Versioning Strategy
URI prefix, header, or both. Deprecation policy.

## Pagination Strategy
Cursor vs offset, page size limits, response shape.

## Response Envelope
Success + error envelope shapes. Top-level keys, error code conventions.

## Filtering, Sorting, Searching
Query parameter conventions for each.

## Identifiers
UUID v4, ULID, snowflake — pick one. Format and visibility rules.

## Error Codes
Standard HTTP status mapping + application-level error code namespace.

## Rate Limits
Default limits per identity tier. Headers returned.

steps.instruction: Merge backend_core_compilation with service designs: Caching Strategy, File Storage, Email System, Search System. Produce complete backend design compilation.
steps.done_when: backend_design_compilation exists as a comprehensive document covering all backend architecture sections.

steps.name: technical_design_document
steps.instruction: Merge backend_design_compilation with frontend architecture, infrastructure, and operational concerns into the final Technical Design Document. Add sections: Frontend Architecture, Observability & Monitoring, Scalability & Disaster Recovery, Data Flow Diagrams, Architecture Decision Records. Include table of contents and version number.
steps.done_when: technical_design_document exists as a comprehensive versioned document.

steps.name: architecture_review
steps.instruction:
Review TDD for: every FR addressable, all NFRs have design elements, no SPOFs, security findings addressed, consistency, feasibility, over-engineering check. Also review auth design, file storage, and other security-sensitive areas specifically.

Z1 Tier 2 (P3+C7+A8) — ADR-aware checks:
- For each ADR JSON under `mission_{mission_id}/.adr/`: the universal shape (adr_id, title, status, context, decision, consequences, options_considered ≥2, chosen_option_id, falsification_signal, reversal_cost, supersedes_adr_id, _schema_version) is fully populated.
- `chosen_option_id` resolves into `options_considered` (no orphan picks).
- `falsification_signal` is non-empty AND measurable (a concrete threshold or signal — not just "if it doesn't work").
- `reversal_cost` is `low | medium | high`.
- Each ADR cites which charter `solution.id` it serves (charter `Solutions We Own`).
- For STACK ADRs (4.2, 4.4, 4.6, 4.8, 4.9, 4.10): every option has a populated `monthly_cost_curve`; the ADR has `cost_at_target_users_usd` (number); reject if `cost_at_target_users_usd > z0.cost_ceiling_monthly_usd` AND `cost_mitigation_plan` is null.
- The component-library ADR from step 4.2a is present and references the tech-stack ADR from 4.2 via `supersedes_adr_id` or an explicit `stack_adr_ref` field.
- Reject the architecture_review_result with `status: fail` if any of the above checks miss; list each failure under `issues[]`.

Z1 Tier 2 (A2) — non-goals contradiction check:
- Read mission_{mission_id}/.charter/non_goals.md if present.
- REJECT (status=fail) any architectural choice or ADR option that maps to a listed non-goal (e.g., non-goal='No real-time features' → reject WebSocket / SSE / event-bus stack picks; non-goal='No mobile-app version' → reject React Native / Flutter ADRs).
- Surface the matching non-goal text in the issue description so the architect can revise.

Z1 Tier 2 (P4) — falsification rigor:
- REJECT ADR options whose chosen rationale lacks risk_if_wrong + validation_method + falsification_signal.
- REJECT when risk_if_wrong='critical' but validation_method is vague.
steps.done_when: architecture_review_result exists with comprehensive assessment.

steps.name: post_architecture_review_green
steps.instruction: Z10 T1A/T3C milestone commit: architecture_review approved => atomic push + mark_green snapshot. Pre-build foundation-confirmed checkpoint.
steps.done_when: Workspace committed (if changes) + remote push succeeded + mission_green_tags row appended.

steps.name: taste_extraction_from_charter
steps.instruction: Read product_charter.md (Solutions, Brand Keywords, JTBD, Guiding Principles). Derive the dominant content-type emphasis the UI must serve and the tone the visual language should carry. Choose ONE primary_content_type from: fact_primary, community_primary, discovery_primary, transactional_primary, informational_primary, other. If 'other', secondary_emphasis MUST be populated. Emit mission_{mission_id}/.style/taste_emphasis.json with this exact shape: {"_schema_version": "1", "mission_id": <int>, "primary_content_type": <slug>, "primary_content_type_rationale": <str>, "secondary_emphasis": [<slug>...], "tone_keywords": [<word>...], "tone_rationale": <str>, "key_visual_priorities": [<word>...], "anti_emphasis": [<word>...]}. Tone keywords are short adjectives (e.g. clinical, playful, minimal, dense, warm, premium). Visual priorities call out what the design must serve (readability, ad-friendly, trust-cues, speed, delight). anti_emphasis lists what we explicitly are NOT (e.g. 'not gamified', 'not ornamental'). _schema_version must be the literal string "1".
steps.done_when: taste_emphasis.json exists at mission_{mission_id}/.style/taste_emphasis.json with all required fields and _schema_version '1'.

steps.name: taste_extraction_verify
steps.instruction: Mechanical shape verification of taste_emphasis.json (required fields, _schema_version, primary_content_type whitelist, 'other' implies populated secondary_emphasis).
steps.done_when: Verifier returns ok=true.

steps.name: design_tokens_generation
steps.instruction: Read .style/taste_emphasis.json and the brand keywords + ambition tier from product_charter.md. Generate mission_{mission_id}/.style/design_tokens.json conditioned on the taste emphasis (NOT a generic palette). Required shape: {"_schema_version": "1", "mission_id": <int>, "variants": {"light": <variant>, "dark": <variant>}, "tag_signature": <primary_content_type slug, e.g. 'fact_primary'>}. Each <variant> MUST contain: color {primary {base, lighter, darker}, background {page, container_primary, container_secondary, container_inset}, text {primary, secondary, tertiary, quaternary, on_dark_primary, on_dark_secondary, link}, functional {urgent_default, urgent_light, neutral_default, neutral_light, opinion_default, opinion_light, success_default, success_light, warning_default, warning_light}, accent {amber|slate|coral or similar named accents}, data_viz [<6 hex colors>]}, typography {font_family_base (real CSS font stack), scale {caption, body_small, body_default, body_emphasized, fact_title, card_title, page_title, display each with size+weight}, line_height}, border_radius {small, medium, large, full}, spacing {base_unit, tight, compact, standard, relaxed}, borders {default, focused}, shadows {none, subtle, moderate, pronounced}, icon_library. All hex values must be real (#RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA), no #XXXXXX, no 'TBD'. tag_signature MUST equal the primary_content_type slug from taste_emphasis. If ambition tier is private_beta or higher, ALSO emit 'compact' and 'comfortable' density variants in variants{}. _schema_version must be the literal string "1".
steps.done_when: design_tokens.json exists with light + dark variants, full token blocks, real hex codes, and tag_signature matching taste_emphasis.primary_content_type.

steps.name: design_tokens_verify
steps.instruction: Mechanical shape verification of design_tokens.json (light + dark present, all required token blocks, no placeholder hex, font_family non-empty, tag_signature present).
steps.done_when: Verifier returns ok=true.

steps.name: surfaces_lock
steps.instruction: Ask founder which surfaces this product targets. Reply keyboard options: 'mobile only', 'web only', 'mobile + web', 'mobile + web + desktop', 'mobile + web + admin'. Persist the decision to mission_{mission_id}/.charter/surfaces.json with schema {_schema_version, mission_id, surfaces, primary_surface, founder_confirmed_at}. primary_surface defaults to first item; founder may override.
steps.done_when: surfaces_config exists with founder confirmation.

steps.name: surfaces_verify
steps.instruction: Mechanical verify of surfaces.json shape. Asserts non-empty surfaces array, valid surface tokens, primary_surface in list.
steps.done_when: surfaces_verify_result.ok == true

steps.name: user_flow_lock
steps.instruction:
Read surfaces.json + product_charter (especially Solutions and persona references). Emit mission_{mission_id}/.flow/user_flow.md with YAML frontmatter (_schema_version='1', mission_id, surfaces) and one ```mermaid graph TD``` block per declared surface. Each node must follow the pattern: Name["Screen Name
/route"]. Match the paraflow shape: per-surface H2 sections with persona name in parentheses; Mobile may split into Authentication/Onboarding + Main App.
steps.done_when: user_flow.md exists with one mermaid block per surface.

steps.name: user_flow_verify
steps.instruction: Mechanical verify of user_flow.md shape. Asserts YAML frontmatter and at least one ```mermaid block per declared surface.
steps.done_when: user_flow_verify_result.ok == true

steps.name: screen_inventory_and_shell
steps.instruction:
Read user_flow.md. Extract every unique screen by parsing mermaid node labels (Name["Screen Name
/route"]). Emit two artifacts: 1. mission_{mission_id}/.flow/screen_inventory.md — YAML frontmatter with total_screens, chunk_size=4 (B8 cohesion ceiling), chunks (list of lists, each <= chunk_size). Body groups screens by surface (## Mobile / ## Web / etc.). Each screen line: '- Screen Name (`/route`)'. 2. mission_{mission_id}/.flow/shared_shell.md — YAML frontmatter with shared_components dict + applicable_to_surfaces. Body has H2 sections for at minimum: Header, EmptyState, ErrorState, LoadingState (TabBar/Footer optional but recommended). Each section describes the invariant pattern.
steps.done_when: screen_inventory and shared_shell both exist.

steps.name: screen_inventory_and_shell_verify
steps.instruction: Mechanical verify of screen_inventory.md (chunks math, every screen has route) AND shared_shell.md (header + empty + error + loading present).
steps.done_when: both verifies pass

steps.name: shared_shell_verify
steps.instruction: Mechanical verify of shared_shell.md shape (frontmatter + header/empty/error/loading sections present).
steps.done_when: shared_shell_verify_result.ok == true

steps.name: information_architecture
steps.instruction: Define app's information architecture: sitemap, content grouping, navigation model, page types.
steps.done_when: information_architecture exists.

steps.name: navigation_design
steps.instruction: Design navigation: primary, secondary, user menu, notifications, search, breadcrumbs, mobile, keyboard shortcuts, deep linking, back behavior.
steps.done_when: navigation_design exists.

steps.name: user_flows
steps.instruction: Diagram all user flows screen-by-screen. Auth flows: signup email, signup oauth, login email, login oauth, password reset, logout. Onboarding: account creation to first value moment, under 2 minutes, including abandonment handling and progressive disclosure. Core features: for each core feature, diagram complete user flow with all screens, transitions, success/error/loading/empty states, confirmations, undo support. Settings: profile, password change, email change, notifications, billing, payment, account deletion, connected accounts.
steps.done_when: All four user flow artifacts exist with screen-by-screen diagrams.

steps.name: layout_and_wireframes
steps.instruction: Define page layout system: app shell, layout templates, grid system, spacing scale, responsive breakpoints, per-breakpoint changes. Then create wireframe specifications for every unique screen in the MVP: content zones, element hierarchy, interactive elements, conditional elements.
steps.done_when: layout_system_spec and wireframe_specs exist.

steps.name: forms_and_states
steps.instruction: Based on wireframe_specs, define every form in the app: fields with validation rules, field grouping, multi-step config if applicable, submit/cancel behavior, auto-save, keyboard behavior. Then define all empty states (first-time, search no results, filtered no results, list empty, offline) and error states (404, 403, 500, maintenance, session expired, rate limited).
steps.done_when: form_specs and empty_error_state_specs exist.

steps.name: wireframe_review
steps.instruction: Review wireframes against user stories and flows: coverage, dead ends, pattern consistency, form validation, empty/error states, navigation reachability.
steps.done_when: wireframe_review_result exists.

steps.name: brand_and_design_tokens
steps.instruction: Define brand identity: personality, tone of voice, color psychology, typography personality, visual style direction. If multiple valid directions, trigger needs_clarification. Then define all design tokens as exact values: colors (primary 50-950, semantic, background, text, border), typography, spacing, borders, shadows, transitions, z-index. All in hex/px/rem.
steps.done_when: brand_identity and design_tokens exist with complete specifications.

steps.name: component_specs
steps.instruction: Specify every primitive component: Button, Input, Textarea, Select, Checkbox, Radio, Toggle, Label, Badge, Avatar, Icon, Divider, Spinner, Skeleton, Link, Tag. Each with variants, sizes, states, props, accessibility, responsive behavior. Then specify composite components: Card, Modal, Toast, Alert, Dropdown, Tooltip, Popover, Tabs, Accordion, Table, Pagination, Breadcrumb, EmptyState, Sidebar, Navbar, Footer. Each with composition from primitives, variants, sizes, states, props.
steps.done_when: component_spec_primitives and component_spec_composite exist with all component specifications.

steps.name: screen_specifications
steps.instruction: For every screen: component tree, data bindings, action handlers, conditional rendering, loading/error states, responsive overrides.
steps.done_when: screen_specifications exists.

steps.name: interaction_responsive_accessibility_specs
steps.instruction: Define all micro-interactions: transitions, feedback, loading behavior, scroll behavior, drag behavior. Include reduced-motion behavior. For each screen at every breakpoint: layout changes, hidden elements, navigation changes, component swaps, touch adaptations, font adjustments, image handling. Per-screen accessibility: focus order, ARIA labels, live regions, keyboard shortcuts, skip links, landmarks, heading hierarchy, form accessibility, color independence.
steps.done_when: interaction_spec, responsive_spec, and accessibility_spec exist.

steps.name: design_review
steps.instruction: Review design specs for coverage, consistency, component reuse, accessibility completeness, responsive completeness, implementability, token compliance. Evaluate design token choices for color harmony, typography hierarchy, spacing consistency, competitive positioning, visual coherence. ALSO verify the Z1 Tier 3 design-tokens cluster: (a) mission_{mission_id}/.style/taste_emphasis.json exists and primary_content_type is populated (and if 'other', secondary_emphasis is non-empty); (b) mission_{mission_id}/.style/design_tokens.json exists with BOTH light and dark variants; (c) all token values reference real concrete values — reject any 'TBD', '#XXXXXX', or placeholder strings; (d) tag_signature field is present in design_tokens.json and matches taste_emphasis.primary_content_type. Reject if any of (a)-(d) fail. Z1 Tier 2 (A2) — non-goals contradiction check: - Read mission_{mission_id}/.charter/non_goals.md if present. - REJECT (status=fail) any feature design that maps to a listed non-goal (e.g., non-goal='No mobile-app version' → reject mobile-only screen specs; non-goal='No support for under-13 users' → reject child-flavored UI patterns). - Surface the matching non-goal text in the issue description so the designer can revise.
