Z1 Tier 3 (C3+A10+C9+A11+C14+C18) — per-screen plan + HTML prototype review (applies when legacy_pre_per_screen_plans is 0): - REJECT (status=fail) when any screen in `screen_inventory.md` lacks a corresponding `mission_{mission_id}/.screens//screen_plan.md` AND `mission_{mission_id}/.web/.html` on disk. - REJECT when a screen plan does not declare ALL FOUR `## States` H3 sub-sections (Default / Empty / Loading / Error). - REJECT when an HTML prototype omits ``, the 390x844 viewport, the Tailwind script tag, an Iconify script tag, or any `` lacks a non-empty descriptive `alt`. - REJECT when an HTML uses a color value not present in `design_tokens.json`. - REJECT when chunk's screens declare inconsistent `inherits_shell` without an explicit `` comment. Surface the offending screen_id + path + concrete failure in the issue list.
steps.done_when: design_review_result exists.

steps.name: design_system_handoff
steps.instruction:
Compile the design system handoff as a single Markdown document.

OUTPUT FORMAT: Return ONLY the Markdown content directly in `result` (no JSON wrapping, no `final_answer` envelope, no `Wrote ...` summary). The Markdown MUST contain these three top-level sections in order, each as a `##` heading:

## Design Tokens
Colors, typography, spacing, borders, shadows, transitions, z-index. List token names with values.

## Primitive Components
Button, Input, Textarea, Select, Checkbox, Radio, Toggle, Label, Badge, Avatar, Icon, Divider, Spinner, Skeleton, Link, Tag. For each: variants, states, props, accessibility notes.

## Composite Components
Card, Modal, Toast, Alert, Dropdown, Tooltip, Popover, Tabs, Accordion, Table, Pagination, Breadcrumb, EmptyState, Sidebar, Navbar, Footer. For each: composition, behavior, accessibility notes.

Then a short `## Review Notes` section pulling key findings from design_review_result.
steps.done_when: design_system_compilation is a Markdown document with `## Design Tokens`, `## Primitive Components`, `## Composite Components` headings present.

steps.name: design_handoff_document
steps.instruction:
Merge design_system_compilation with screen specs, interaction patterns, responsive rules, and accessibility requirements into the final Design Handoff Document.

OUTPUT FORMAT: Return ONLY the Markdown content directly in `result` (no JSON wrapping, no `final_answer` envelope, no `Wrote ...` summary). The Markdown MUST contain these top-level sections in order, each as a `##` heading:

## Design Tokens
Key tokens from design_system_compilation.

## Component Library
Primitive + composite component summaries from design_system_compilation.

## Screen Specifications
Key screens from screen_specifications.

## Interaction Guide
Micro-interaction + animation rules from interaction_spec.

## Responsive Guide
Breakpoint behavior from responsive_spec.

## Accessibility Guide
WCAG-aligned guidelines from accessibility_spec.
steps.done_when: design_handoff is a Markdown document with `## Design Tokens`, `## Component Library`, `## Screen Specifications`, `## Interaction Guide`, `## Responsive Guide`, `## Accessibility Guide` headings present.

steps.name: generate_per_screen_plans_chunk_a
steps.instruction:
Z1 Tier 3 (C3+A10+C14): generate paraflow-shape per-screen plans for the FIRST chunk of `screen_inventory.chunks` (B8 cohesion ceiling: 3-5 screens). For EVERY screen in this chunk, write a Markdown file to `mission_{mission_id}/.screens/<slug>/screen_plan.md` with this STRICT shape:

1. YAML frontmatter delimited by `---` carrying:
   - `_schema_version: "1"`
   - `mission_id: <id>`
   - `screen_id: <slug>`
   - `route: "/..."`
   - `surface: mobile|web|desktop` (from surfaces.md)
   - `inherits_shell: [...]` (subset of components in shared_shell.md; same list across the whole chunk unless you also emit `<!-- inherits_shell_override: <reason> -->` in the body)
2. `# <ScreenName>` H1 + one-line description paragraph (what this screen lets the user accomplish).
3. At least one `## <Section>` H2 beyond `## States` describing the screen's content blocks (e.g. `## Search Bar`, `## Featured Content`, `## Quick Actions`, `## TabBar`). Bullet lists per section.
4. `## States` H2 with EXACTLY four H3 sub-sections in order: `### Default`, `### Empty`, `### Loading`, `### Error`. Each describes the visual state with bullet details (skeleton hints for Loading; retry CTA for Error; hero illustration + CTA for Empty).

OUTPUT FORMAT: emit ONE Markdown file per screen via `write_file` to the path above. Your final_answer payload is `per_screen_plans_chunk_a` JSON object listing the produced paths: `{"_schema_version": "1", "chunk": "a", "plans": [{"screen_id": "home", "path": "mission_{mission_id}/.screens/home/screen_plan.md"}, ...]}`. The mechanical post-hook `verify_screen_plan_shape` rejects each plan if the frontmatter / description / section / States contract is not met.
steps.done_when: per_screen_plans_chunk_a JSON lists at least 1 produced plan path AND every listed file exists on disk.

steps.name: verify_screen_plan_shape_chunk_a
steps.instruction: Mechanical sibling — assert paraflow shape on every plan produced by 5.20a. Caller wires `plan_paths` from the 5.20a artifact's `plans[].path` list.

steps.name: generate_per_screen_plans_chunk_b
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 3 (C3+A10+C14): generate paraflow-shape per-screen plans for the SECOND chunk of `screen_inventory.chunks`. Same shape contract as 5.20a. Write each plan to `mission_{mission_id}/.screens/<slug>/screen_plan.md`. Inherit shared_shell decisions from chunk_a unless you have a screen-specific reason — emit `<!-- inherits_shell_override: <reason> -->` in the body when diverging. Final_answer is `per_screen_plans_chunk_b` JSON object listing produced paths. If `screen_inventory.chunks` has only one chunk, return `{"_schema_version": "1", "chunk": "b", "plans": []}`.
steps.done_when: per_screen_plans_chunk_b is a JSON object with `chunk` + `plans` keys.

steps.name: verify_screen_plan_shape_chunk_b
steps.instruction: Mechanical sibling — assert paraflow shape on every plan produced by 5.20b.

steps.name: verify_screen_plan_consistency
steps.instruction: Mechanical sibling — cross-screen `inherits_shell` consistency check across both chunks (B8/C18). Caller wires `screen_plan_paths` (union of both chunks' plans) and `shared_shell_components` from shared_shell.md.

steps.name: generate_html_prototypes_chunk_a
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 3 (C9+A11): for every screen plan in `per_screen_plans_chunk_a`, generate a paraflow-shape mobile HTML prototype at `mission_{mission_id}/.web/.html`. STRICT contract: 1. `` at the top. 2. Mobile viewport 390x844 — body must carry Tailwind `w-[390px] min-h-[844px]` (or inline `width:390px; min-height:844px`). 3. Tailwind via CDN: ``. 4. Iconify-Lucide via CDN: `` and reference icons as ``. 5. Colors: ONLY use values present in `design_tokens.json` (read at gen time and inline as hex/rgb literals). NEVER invent new color values. 6. Images: PLACEHOLDER ONLY — every `` src must use `https://placehold.co/x//?text=`. Z2 `gorsel_ustasi` swaps to real images later. Each `` MUST carry a descriptive `alt="..."` attribute that describes what the real image should show — this becomes the prompt for image gen. 7. Apply `shared_shell.md` components consistently — the same header / nav / tab-bar markup across every screen in the chunk. OUTPUT FORMAT: emit ONE HTML file per screen via `write_file`. Your final_answer is `html_prototypes_chunk_a` JSON object listing produced paths: `{"_schema_version": "1", "chunk": "a", "prototypes": [{"screen_id": "home", "path": "mission_{mission_id}/.web/home.html"}, ...]}`. Mechanical post-hook `verify_html_prototype_shape` rejects any HTML missing the contract above.
steps.done_when: html_prototypes_chunk_a JSON lists at least 1 produced HTML path AND every listed file exists on disk.

steps.name: verify_html_prototype_shape_chunk_a
steps.instruction: Mechanical sibling — assert paraflow shape on every HTML produced by 5.30a. Caller wires `html_paths` and `design_tokens` (parsed JSON) from the artifact store.

steps.name: generate_html_prototypes_chunk_b
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 3 (C9+A11): generate HTML prototypes for the SECOND chunk's screens. Same contract as 5.30a (DOCTYPE / 390x844 / Tailwind / Iconify / design_tokens-only colors / placehold.co images with descriptive alt / shared_shell). Match chunk_a's stylistic decisions. If `per_screen_plans_chunk_b.plans` is empty, return `{"_schema_version": "1", "chunk": "b", "prototypes": []}`.
steps.done_when: html_prototypes_chunk_b is a JSON object with `chunk` + `prototypes` keys.

steps.name: verify_html_prototype_shape_chunk_b
steps.instruction: Mechanical sibling — assert paraflow shape on every HTML produced by 5.30b.

steps.name: annotate_html_oids
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 4 (T4B / C17+A20): post-process every HTML emitted by 5.30a/b — walk the DOM via BeautifulSoup and assign `data-oid="<artifact_slug>:<section>"` to semantic blocks (header/main/nav/footer/aside/section/article + role-classed divs: hero/cta/card/form/etc.). Anchor for `propose_spec_patch_from_html_diff`. Caller wires `html_paths` (union of both chunks) and `artifact_slug` derived per file. Idempotent: existing `data-oid` values are preserved.

steps.name: emit_preview_url
steps.instruction: Z1 T4C (C10+A19) — emit a tunneled preview URL surface for the per-screen HTML prototypes. EMIT-ONLY: writes preview_url.txt with a live cloudflared URL when KUTAI_PREVIEW_PROVIDER=cloudflared and the binary is on PATH; otherwise writes a 'pending: hosting deferred to Z2' placeholder. Idempotent: any prior tunnel is terminated before re-emitting.

steps.name: epic_definition_and_work_breakdown
steps.done_when: JSON object with `epics` array (>=1 with epic_id/name/user_stories/dependencies/priority_order) and `work_breakdown.tasks` array.

steps.name: dependency_graph_and_estimation
steps.instruction: Build complete task dependency graph. Identify: critical_path, parallelizable_groups, bottleneck_tasks. Then refine effort estimates (XS/S/M/L/XL) with confidence levels. Flag XL tasks for decomposition. Calculate totals and identify risky estimates.
steps.done_when: dependency_graph and effort_estimates exist.

steps.name: risk_assessment
steps.instruction: Identify risks across: technical, integration, scope, dependency, security, operational. Each with: risk_id, description, category, likelihood, impact, risk_score, early_warning_signs, mitigation_strategy, contingency_plan.
steps.done_when: risk_assessment exists with all risks scored, with mitigation and contingency plans.

steps.name: sprint_planning
steps.done_when: JSON object with `sprint_0_plan` (containing `tasks` and `order`) and `sprint_plans` array (>=1 sprint with sprint_id/tasks/deliverable).

steps.name: milestones_and_dod
steps.instruction: Define milestones: alpha, beta, release_candidate, general_availability. Each with target_sprint, entry/exit criteria, risks_to_timeline. Define DoD at: task (code, lint, type check, tests, self-review), story (all tasks, acceptance criteria, code review, no critical bugs, deployed to staging), sprint (all stories, regression suite, no critical bugs, stable staging), release (all sprints, security audit, perf targets, docs, legal, infra).
steps.done_when: milestones and definition_of_done exist.

steps.name: failure_premortem
steps.done_when: premortem.md exists at mission_{mission_id}/premortem.md, contains a JSON envelope with >=3 scenarios covering all of {technical, market, founder}, every plausibility ∈ [1,5].

steps.name: verify_premortem_shape
steps.instruction: Mechanical post-hook on 6.5z. Caller wires `premortem_path` (default `mission_{mission_id}/premortem.md`) or `premortem_text`. Asserts the JSON envelope: _schema_version=='1', scenarios>=3, kinds {technical, market, founder} each present, plausibility ∈ [1,5], obituary + cause non-empty.

steps.name: project_plan_review
steps.instruction:
Review project plan: all stories assigned, dependencies respected, balanced loads, realistic milestones, risk mitigations as tasks, testing in every sprint, buffer exists. If timeline unrealistic, trigger needs_clarification. Z1 T5A (P6): also verify compliance_blocker_check did not surface pending docs for phase ≤6.

Z1 Tier 5 (A6) PREMORTEM CHECK — after the standard plan review, also examine the `premortem` artifact. For EVERY scenario with `plausibility >= 4`, confirm a corresponding monitoring rule exists (either `mapped_monitoring_rule` is non-null AND points to a real rule_id, or risk_assessment / monitoring inventory contains a rule that would catch the named cause). If a high-plausibility scenario has no monitoring coverage, REJECT the review (status != pass) with an `issues` entry naming the unmapped scenario; surface as needs_clarification so the founder either accepts the gap explicitly or amends monitoring.
steps.done_when: project_plan_review_result exists. Timeline is realistic or human has approved adjusted scope.

steps.name: init_github_repo
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 6 (C18) — at the end of phase 6 (after compliance_blocker_check on 6.6 has passed) initialise a GitHub repository for the mission via gh-cli and push the locked phase-≤6 spec artifacts as the initial commit. Fail-soft when gh-cli is missing or unauthenticated: writes `mission_{mission_id}/github_init_status.md` with `pending: <reason>` and the workflow continues — founder retries via Telegram `/github init <mission_id>`. Repo name: `kutai-mission-<id>-<charter_slug>` (kebab-case, ≤30 chars from charter title). Visibility default `public` (overridable via `KUTAI_GITHUB_DEFAULT_VISIBILITY`). Owner default = current `gh` user (overridable via `KUTAI_GITHUB_ORG`). The export uses a separate `mission_{mission_id}/.git_export/` repo so any mission-internal git history (created later in phase 7+) is not clobbered. Set `payload.skip_github=true` to opt out (still emits the status file). Persists `missions.github_repo_url`.

steps.name: index_mission_artifacts
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 6A (P9) — walk phase-≤6 artifacts (charter, compliance_fingerprint, compliance_overlay, ADR register + .adrs/*.json, non_goals.md, reverse_pitch.md), read each `_schema_version` envelope, derive domain_keywords from the charter brand keywords, and insert/upsert one row per artifact into `mission_artifacts_index`. Cross-mission inheritance hints at step 0.5 of future missions read from this index.
steps.done_when: mission_artifacts_index has rows for the in-flight mission's available phase-≤6 artifacts.

steps.name: spec_consistency_check_phase_7
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 5B (B5) — Augment Intent's `specs stay alive`. Wave-start mechanical step before phase-7 execution. Re-reads phase-≤6 locked spec artifacts (charter, ADRs, design_tokens, screen_plans, surfaces, non_goals, premortem, compliance_overlay if present) + any phase-7 artifacts on disk; rule-based drift scan (R1 stack drift / R2 token drift / R3 surface drift / R4 non-goal drift). On drift, returns needs_review + drift report path; reviewer / source has to acknowledge before downstream waves continue.

steps.name: record_hypothesis
steps.instruction: Z9 T4A — at Phase 7 spec finalization, capture the mission's predicted metric impact as a pending hypotheses row so a later verdict can check whether the prediction held. Deterministic spec parsing (regex/heuristic for '<metric> <direction> <magnitude>'); window resolved via per-metric default table (activation/acquisition 7d, retention 30d, revenue/referral 14d, latency/error_rate 3d, default 14d). Pure refactor / doc-only missions are flagged (hypothesis_skipped growth_event) but not blocked. NO LLM.
steps.done_when: A hypotheses row exists for this mission, OR the mission was flagged non-measurable / suppressed (result.ok == true in all cases).

steps.name: stripe_scaffold
steps.instruction: Z6 T5A — emit checkout + webhook + .env.example + README scaffold under `mission_{mission_id}/api/` when monetization_strategy calls for Stripe billing. The executor self-skips when `billing.provider != stripe` AND no priced `products[]` array is present. Default stack is Python+FastAPI; TS/Node deferred to a future tier.
steps.done_when: stripe_scaffold_result.ok == true (skipped is also a pass).

steps.name: repository_setup
steps.instruction: Initialize the repository using the scaffold tool if applicable. Create the repository structure, .gitignore for the detected tech stack, .editorconfig, README stub. Initialize git, set up branching strategy (main + develop), make initial commit. Configure git hooks directory.
steps.done_when: repository_created and git_config_files exist with initialized repo and git configuration.

steps.name: linting_and_formatting
steps.instruction: Set up code formatting (Prettier/Black/rustfmt), linting (ESLint/Pylint/clippy), and pre-commit hooks (husky/pre-commit). Configure all tools with project-appropriate rules. Set up editor configuration files (.vscode/settings.json, .editorconfig). Ensure pre-commit hooks run formatter and linter.
steps.done_when: linter_config, formatter_config, and precommit_config exist and pre-commit hooks run correctly.

steps.name: backend_scaffold
steps.instruction:
Use the scaffold tool to initialize the backend project with the selected framework. Set up: project structure matching system_architecture, TypeScript/language configuration (strict mode), environment management (.env.example with all required vars), base middleware (CORS, body parsing, request logging per observability_strategy, error handling per observability_strategy), API validation library, health check endpoint. Install and verify all dependencies.

AFTER the scaffold work is done, RETURN a JSON object directly in `result` (no envelope, no markdown, no "Wrote ..." summary):

{
  "project_path": "<absolute or workspace-relative path of scaffolded project>",
  "dependencies_installed": true,
  "health_check_verified": true,
  "framework": "<framework name + version>",
  "notes": "<one paragraph: what was set up, anything skipped or deferred>"
}

Set boolean fields to true ONLY when actually verified (dependency install command exited 0; curl to health endpoint returned 200). If verification couldn't run (e.g. tool unavailable), still emit the field as false with notes explaining.
steps.done_when: backend_scaffold exists with a running backend that responds to health checks.

steps.name: backend_scaffold_git_commit
steps.instruction: Mechanical auto-commit after 7.3. Z10 T1A: payload.push=true => atomic commit↔push (rollback local commit if push fails).
steps.done_when: Workspace committed if there are changes.

steps.name: database_setup
steps.done_when: db_client, migration_system, and seed_script exist and work correctly.

steps.name: frontend_scaffold
steps.instruction:
Use the scaffold tool to initialize the frontend project with the selected framework. Set up: project structure matching frontend_architecture, TypeScript configuration (strict mode), routing configuration per information_architecture, state management per frontend_architecture, API client with auth token handling and error handling per observability_strategy. Install and verify all dependencies.

AFTER the scaffold work is done, RETURN a JSON object directly in `result` (no envelope, no markdown, no "Wrote ..." summary):

{
  "project_path": "<absolute or workspace-relative path of scaffolded project>",
  "dependencies_installed": true,
  "dev_server_verified": true,
  "framework": "<framework name + version>",
  "notes": "<one paragraph: what was set up, anything skipped or deferred>"
}

Set boolean fields to true ONLY when actually verified (npm/pnpm install exited 0; dev server started and responded). If verification couldn't run (e.g. tool unavailable), still emit the field as false with notes explaining.
steps.done_when: frontend_scaffold exists with a running frontend dev server.

steps.name: frontend_scaffold_git_commit
steps.instruction: Mechanical auto-commit after 7.5. Z10 T1A push=true => atomic commit↔push. Z10 T3C mark_green=true => snapshot mission DB + Chroma + git tag for /rollback_mission.
steps.done_when: Workspace committed if there are changes.

steps.name: test_infrastructure
steps.done_when: JSON object with `backend_test_config` and `frontend_test_config` each carrying framework + config_file + example_test_passes.

steps.name: docker_setup
steps.instruction: Create Docker setup: backend Dockerfile (multi-stage build), frontend Dockerfile (multi-stage build), docker-compose.yml with all services (backend, frontend, database, cache if needed). Configure volumes, networking, environment variables, and health checks. Verify the full stack starts and runs with docker-compose up.
steps.done_when: docker_compose_config exists and docker-compose up works.

steps.name: ci_cd_pipeline
steps.done_when: JSON object with `ci_config` (provider/config_file/stages), `cd_staging_config` (trigger/config), `cd_production_config` (trigger/config).

steps.name: design_system_implementation
steps.instruction: Implement design tokens as CSS variables/theme configuration and the base layout system. Convert all design tokens to code (CSS custom properties, Tailwind theme, or styled-system). Implement the app shell layout, responsive grid system, and base navigation components. Verify all tokens render correctly at all breakpoints.
steps.done_when: design_tokens_code and layout_components exist with verified rendering.

steps.name: primitive_components
steps.instruction: Implement all primitive components from component_spec_primitives: Button, Input, Textarea, Select, Checkbox, Radio, Toggle, Label, Badge, Avatar, Icon, Divider, Spinner, Skeleton, Link, Tag. Each with all variants, sizes, states, and accessibility attributes. Use design tokens for all styling.
steps.done_when: primitive_components_code exists with all primitives implemented.

steps.name: composite_components
steps.instruction: Implement all composite components from component_spec_composite: Card, Modal, Toast, Alert, Dropdown, Tooltip, Popover, Tabs, Accordion, Table, Pagination, Breadcrumb, EmptyState, Sidebar, Navbar, Footer. Compose from primitive components. Include animations per interaction_spec.
steps.done_when: composite_components_code exists with all composites implemented.

steps.name: component_playground
steps.instruction: Set up a component playground (Storybook, Ladle, or equivalent) with stories for every primitive and composite component. Each story should show all variants, sizes, and states.
steps.done_when: storybook_setup exists with stories for all components.

steps.name: staging_environment
steps.instruction: [NEEDS-REAL-TOOLS] Requires real deploy adapters (Vercel/Railway/Fly/Render/etc.) the agent does not currently have. Until those adapters land in mr_roboto, do NOT autonomously execute this step — surface a needs_clarification asking the human operator to provision and report back, then record their answer as the artifact. Provision staging environment and deploy the scaffolded application. Verify health checks pass, database migrations run, and the application is accessible. Test the CD pipeline by triggering a deployment.
steps.done_when: staging_environment and staging_deployment_verified exist.

steps.name: sprint_0_verification
steps.instruction: Run comprehensive smoke tests on the staging environment: verify backend health endpoint, verify frontend loads, verify database connection, verify docker-compose works locally, verify CI pipeline passes, verify linting/formatting runs.
steps.done_when: sprint_0_smoke_result exists with all checks passing.

steps.name: developer_workflow_docs
steps.instruction: Write developer workflow documentation covering: how to set up the local development environment, how to run the application, how to run tests, how to create a new feature branch, how to deploy to staging, coding conventions to follow.
steps.done_when: developer_workflow_doc exists.

steps.name: sprint_0_review
steps.instruction: Review all Sprint 0 deliverables: repository structure, CI/CD pipelines, Docker setup, design system components, test infrastructure, staging environment, developer documentation. Verify everything works end-to-end. Run index_workspace to build the codebase index â€” this enables query_codebase for all subsequent implementation steps.
steps.done_when: sprint_0_review_result exists.

steps.name: implementation_context_package
steps.instruction: Create a condensed implementation reference. For each major subsystem: the 3-5 most important design decisions, the specific patterns to follow, the key constraints. This document replaces the need for implementer agents to read the full TDD. Target: under 5000 words covering everything an implementer needs for day-to-day coding decisions.
steps.done_when: implementation_context exists as a concise (<5000 words) reference for all implementation work.

steps.name: spec_consistency_check_phase_8
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 5B (B5) — wave-start spec_consistency_check at the head of phase 8. See 7.0z for full description.

steps.name: inject_north_star
steps.instruction: Z9 T4B — read the success_metrics artifact (created at step 2.9, otherwise never re-read) and merge north_star_metric + aarrr_metrics into mission.context['north_star'] so Phase 8+ feature-scoring steps (implementation backlog, recipe choice, mission ranking) see the product's north-star. Mirrors inject_lessons. Idempotent; graceful when no artifact (injected=false). NO LLM.
steps.done_when: result.ok == true (injected=true when a success_metrics artifact exists, injected=false is also a pass).

steps.name: assign_ab_variants
steps.instruction: Z9 T5D — A/B is default-on for every Phase 8+ feature mission. Split the mission into control + treatment variants in experiment_variants, wire a 50/50 PostHog multivariate feature-flag via vendor_call, and attach the variants to the Phase-7 hypothesis (7.0y). Insufficient-N guard: if PostHog daily-active < 100 the split is skipped (ab_skipped_low_n growth_event), the feature ships at 100% rollout and the hypothesis still stands. Honours mission.context['use_ab']=false (set by /experiment_disable) as an opt-out. The Bayesian winner is computed later by the verdict pipeline; auto-rollback of a loser still needs a founder /experiment_ship or /experiment_rollback. NO LLM.
steps.done_when: result.ok == true (split=true assigns variants; split=false is also a pass when insufficient-N or opted out).

steps.name: validate_target_segment
steps.instruction: Z9 T5A — cohort-awareness nag. Check mission.context['target_segment'] ('paid_users' | 'new_signups' | 'week2_churners' | 'any', set at i2p step 2.9). When a Phase 8+ mission reaches the implementation backlog with no explicit target_segment, emit a WARNING (never a block) and back-fill the explicit 'any' default. A growth change scoped to everyone is rarely intentional. NO LLM.
steps.done_when: result.ok == true (warned=true when target_segment was absent is still a pass — this is a warn-only nag, never a block).

steps.name: implementation_backlog_initialization
steps.instruction: Initialize the executable implementation backlog. Transform sprint_plans into a flat, ordered list of features to implement. The backlog MUST cover EVERY feature from mvp_scope.mvp_feature_list — do not collapse, deduplicate, or drop entries. Each backlog entry: feature_id (matching pattern F-NNN, e.g. F-001), feature_name (the human-readable name from mvp_scope, min 3 chars), epic_id, sprint_id, user_story_ids, depends_on_features, status (pending/in_progress/completed/blocked), template_ref ('feature_implementation_template'). Order respecting dependencies and sprint assignments. feature_id values MUST be unique across the backlog.
steps.done_when: implementation_backlog exists as an ordered list of features covering every mvp_feature_list entry, each referencing the implementation template, with unique F-NNN ids.

steps.name: recipe_pick_all
steps.instruction: Pick recipes for all features in implementation_backlog. For each backlog entry, call pick_recipe with the feature name and detected tech stack. Emit recipe_picks.json mapping feature_name to pick result.
steps.done_when: recipe_picks.json written with entries for all backlog features (null allowed for no match).

steps.name: instantiate_picked_recipes
steps.instruction: Read recipe_picks.json; for each non-null pick, instantiate the recipe templates into the mission workspace under {feature_slug}/. Emit recipe_instantiations.json listing files written per feature.
steps.done_when: recipe_instantiations.json written; instantiation entries non-empty for every non-null pick.

steps.name: feasibility_spike
steps.instruction: Execute feasibility spikes for the highest-risk items identified in risk_assessment. For each spike: build a minimal proof-of-concept testing the risky integration or architecture decision. Verify: third-party APIs work as documented, performance-critical paths meet NFR targets at prototype scale, complex algorithms produce correct results. If any spike fails, trigger needs_clarification with alternatives. Document findings and any design adjustments needed.
steps.done_when: spike_results exists with proof-of-concept results for all high-risk items.

steps.name: feasibility_spike_git_commit
steps.instruction: Mechanical auto-commit after 8.spike. Z10 T1A push=true => atomic commit↔push. Z10 T3C mark_green=true => post-spike green checkpoint (architecture validated, before full build).
steps.done_when: Workspace committed if there are changes.

steps.name: sprint_boundary_check
steps.instruction: Sprint boundary checkpoint. Execute when all features assigned to current sprint are completed. Summarize what was built, run regression suite, compare velocity, identify slips, review next sprint. If velocity >30% below plan, trigger needs_clarification. Output: sprint_id_completed, features_completed, velocity_ratio, next_sprint_adjustments.
steps.done_when: Sprint is reviewed, next sprint is confirmed or adjusted.
steps.trigger: Execute after the last feature of each sprint completes

steps.name: architecture_alignment_check
steps.instruction: Compare what was actually built this sprint against the technical_design_document. Identify deviations classified as: intentional_improvement (keep code, update design doc), accidental_drift (fix code to match design), design_was_wrong (design needs to change to match reality). For 'design_was_wrong' items: write a mini-ADR documenting what changed and why, update the relevant section of technical_design_document in-place.
steps.done_when: architecture_deviation_report exists and any design doc updates have been applied.
steps.trigger: Execute at every sprint boundary alongside sprint_boundary_check

steps.name: spec_consistency_check_phase_9
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 5B (B5) — wave-start spec_consistency_check at the head of phase 9. See 7.0z for full description.

steps.name: coverage_analysis
steps.instruction: Run the project's test suite with coverage reporting enabled. Analyze the coverage output to identify which modules, functions, and branches are untested. Produce a coverage_report listing: overall line/branch/function coverage percentages, per-module coverage breakdown, and a prioritized list of uncovered critical paths (auth, payment, data mutations).
steps.done_when: coverage_report exists.

steps.name: test_gap_filling
steps.instruction: Fill testing gaps identified in coverage_report. Write critical path tests for login, signup, checkout, data CRUD, permission checks â€” happy path and primary failure mode. Write negative scenario tests: invalid inputs, unauthorized access, expired tokens, malformed requests, missing required fields, rate limits. Write boundary value tests for all numeric inputs, string length limits, date ranges, pagination parameters, file size limits â€” test zero, one, max, max+1, negative, empty. Follow project test patterns.
steps.done_when: critical_path_tests, negative_tests, and boundary_tests exist.

steps.name: concurrency_and_integrity_tests
steps.instruction: Write concurrency tests: simultaneous writes to the same resource, concurrent user sessions, parallel API requests to stateful endpoints, database transaction conflicts. Write data integrity tests: foreign key constraints, cascading deletes, unique constraints, required fields, data type validations at DB level, transaction rollback.
steps.done_when: concurrency_tests and integrity_tests exist.

steps.name: e2e_test_suite
steps.instruction: Author E2E test files only. Do NOT claim execution results — a sibling mechanical step (9.4a) runs the suite via mr_roboto.run_pytest and emits the actual pass/fail. Review existing E2E tests against functional requirements. Write missing E2E tests for user flows without coverage. Each test should simulate a complete user flow from entry to completion, including UI interactions, API calls, and database state verification. Use the project's E2E framework (Playwright, Cypress, or equivalent). Emit `test_files` (list of paths under `e2e/`) and `flows_covered` (count of authored test cases). Do NOT emit `flows_passed` — that's the runner's job.
steps.done_when: e2e_test_suite exists with authored test files.

steps.name: e2e_test_run
steps.instruction: Mechanical: run the authored E2E suite via mr_roboto.run_pytest against the e2e/ directory. Pass requires pytest exit 0, total > 0, failed == 0, errors == 0. A green exit on zero-collected counts as failure (silent skip).
steps.done_when: e2e_test_run_result.ok is true.

steps.name: performance_testing
steps.instruction: Create performance test scripts using k6, Artillery, or equivalent. Write scripts for: API endpoint response time benchmarks, database query performance under load, concurrent user simulation (10, 50, 100, 500 users), throughput tests for highest-traffic endpoints. Then run them against staging to establish baseline metrics. Capture p50/p95/p99 response times, throughput, error rates, CPU/memory utilization.
steps.done_when: performance_test_scripts and baseline_performance_results exist.

steps.name: stress_test_and_optimization
steps.instruction: Run stress tests at 2x, 5x, 10x expected load. Identify breaking point and bottlenecks. Then implement optimizations: database indexes, query caching, N+1 fixes, connection pooling, compression, pagination, lazy-loading. Re-run performance tests to verify improvements. Document before/after metrics.
steps.done_when: stress_test_results and performance_optimizations exist with before/after comparison.

steps.name: cross_platform_testing
steps.instruction: Run tests and key user flows in target browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) and on mobile devices/screen sizes (320px, 375px, 414px, 768px). Verify layout, JS functionality, forms, animations, touch interactions, virtual keyboard behavior. Document any failures with screenshots.
steps.done_when: cross_browser_results and mobile_test_results exist.

steps.name: cross_platform_fixes
steps.instruction: Fix all critical and major cross-platform issues. Apply CSS fixes, vendor prefixes, flexbox/grid fallbacks, responsive breakpoint adjustments, touch target sizing. Verify each fix in the affected browser/device.
steps.done_when: cross_platform_fixes exists with all critical/major issues resolved.

steps.name: accessibility_testing_and_fixes
steps.instruction: Run automated accessibility scanning (axe-core, Pa11y, or Lighthouse) against WCAG 2.1 AA. Test keyboard navigation for all interactive elements. Check color contrast for all text/background combinations. Fix all critical and serious violations: missing form labels, keyboard traps, zero-contrast text, missing alt text, broken ARIA. Re-run scans after fixes to verify.
steps.done_when: a11y_scan_results and a11y_fixes_applied exist with all critical/serious issues resolved.

steps.name: full_regression_suite
steps.instruction: Run the complete test suite including unit tests, integration tests, E2E tests, performance tests, and accessibility tests as a full regression after all Phase 9 fixes. All tests must pass. If any fail, investigate whether they are pre-existing flaky tests or regressions from the fixes.
steps.done_when: regression_results exists with all tests passing.

steps.name: bug_compilation
steps.instruction: Compile all bugs discovered during Phase 9 testing into a structured bug_report. For each bug: assign a unique ID, severity (P0-P3), category, reproduction steps, expected vs actual behavior, affected browsers/devices, and suggested fix approach. Sort by severity.
steps.done_when: bug_report exists.

steps.name: spec_consistency_check_phase_10
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 5B (B5) — wave-start spec_consistency_check at the head of phase 10. See 7.0z for full description.

steps.name: owasp_audit
steps.instruction: Conduct a systematic OWASP Top 10 security audit. Review the codebase and running application against each category: Injection, Broken Authentication, Sensitive Data Exposure, XXE, Broken Access Control, Security Misconfiguration, XSS, Insecure Deserialization, Vulnerable Components, Insufficient Logging. For each category, document: risk level, evidence found, and remediation steps. Emit `findings` as an array of {category, severity (critical|high|medium|low|info), description, remediation}; entries at severity critical or high block the gate until resolved (fix the code or document a real mitigation — do NOT just downgrade severity).
steps.done_when: owasp_audit_result exists with no critical/high findings outstanding.

steps.name: injection_and_xss_testing
steps.instruction: Test all user input points for SQL injection, NoSQL injection, LDAP injection, OS command injection, and ORM injection. Test API parameters, query strings, form fields, HTTP headers, file upload filenames. Also test all output points for reflected XSS, stored XSS, and DOM-based XSS. Verify output encoding and Content-Security-Policy headers.
steps.done_when: injection_test_result and xss_test_result exist.

steps.name: auth_and_authz_testing
steps.instruction: Review authentication: password hashing (bcrypt/scrypt/argon2), session tokens (random, long), token expiration/refresh, account lockout, password reset flow. Test authorization: horizontal privilege escalation (user A accessing user B's data), vertical escalation (regular user accessing admin), IDOR vulnerabilities, role-based access at API and data layers. Test CSRF: tokens on all forms, SameSite cookie, custom headers for AJAX.
steps.done_when: auth_security_result, authz_test_result, and csrf_test_result exist.

steps.name: sast_and_dependency_scan
steps.instruction: Run dependency vulnerability scanning (npm audit, pip audit, Snyk, Trivy). Scan codebase and git history for exposed secrets (gitleaks, truffleHog, detect-secrets). Audit HTTP security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy). Review API security: auth on non-public endpoints, request validation, no internal detail leakage, rate limiting, request size limits.
steps.done_when: dependency_scan_result, secrets_audit_result, and security_headers_audit_result exist.

steps.name: encryption_and_logging_review
steps.instruction: Verify encryption: all data in transit uses TLS 1.2+, sensitive data at rest is encrypted, database connections use SSL, encryption keys stored securely, key rotation exists. Review logging security: passwords and tokens never logged, PII masked, log injection prevented, log files have appropriate permissions, error logs don't expose internals. Emit `findings` for each artifact as an array of {check, severity (critical|high|medium|low|info), description, remediation}; entries at severity critical or high block the gate until resolved (fix the underlying issue or document a real mitigation — do NOT just downgrade severity).
steps.done_when: encryption_verification_result and logging_security_result exist with no critical/high findings outstanding.

steps.name: dependency_and_header_fixes
steps.instruction: Fix all critical and high severity dependency vulnerabilities: upgrade to patched versions, assess breaking changes for major upgrades, document accepted risks. Implement all missing or misconfigured security headers: CSP with nonce-based script allowlisting, HSTS with 1-year max-age, all recommended headers. Run test suite after each change.
steps.done_when: dependency_fixes and security_headers_implemented exist.

steps.name: file_upload_security
steps.instruction: Review file upload implementation: server-side type validation, malware scanning, size limits, filename sanitization (path traversal prevention), files stored outside web root, content-disposition headers force download, image re-encoding to strip EXIF/metadata.
steps.done_when: file_upload_security_result exists.

steps.name: security_triage
steps.instruction: Compile all security test results into a prioritized triage list. For each finding: severity (critical/high/medium/low), description, affected component, suggested fix. Sort by severity. Output as structured JSON.
steps.done_when: security_triage exists with all findings prioritized by severity.

steps.name: security_fixes
steps.instruction: Apply fixes for all critical and high severity findings from security_triage. For each fix: describe what was changed, verify the fix doesn't break functionality. Also address any issues from encryption, logging, file upload, and secrets audits.
steps.done_when: security_fixes_applied exists with all critical and high fixes applied.

steps.name: security_retest_and_report
steps.instruction: Re-run all security tests to verify fixes resolved vulnerabilities without introducing new ones. Then write a comprehensive security audit report: executive summary with overall security posture rating, methodology, vulnerabilities found by category and severity, remediation actions taken, current status, remaining risks, and recommendations.
steps.done_when: security_retest_results and security_audit_report exist.

steps.name: spec_consistency_check_phase_11
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 5B (B5) — wave-start spec_consistency_check at the head of phase 11. See 7.0z for full description.

steps.name: api_and_developer_docs
steps.instruction: Generate comprehensive API documentation from openapi_spec. Write a developer setup guide covering: prerequisites, cloning, env vars, dependency installation, database setup, running dev server, running tests, common tasks. Write an architecture overview covering: system diagram, tech stack rationale, data flow, directory structure, key patterns, external integrations.
steps.done_when: api_docs, developer_setup_guide, and architecture_overview exist.

steps.name: user_documentation
steps.instruction: Write a getting-started guide for new users covering signup, initial config, first-time walkthrough, key concepts. Write detailed feature documentation for every MVP feature. Compile a FAQ document (20-30 entries) covering account management, billing, feature capabilities, troubleshooting, privacy/security, and API questions.
steps.done_when: user_getting_started, feature_docs, and faq_doc exist.

steps.name: in_app_copy
steps.instruction: Write all in-app onboarding copy (welcome, tooltips, empty states, progress indicators). Write user-friendly error messages for every error state. Write copy for all notification templates (transactional emails, in-app notifications). Format as structured JSON files keyed by screen/component ID.
steps.done_when: onboarding_copy, error_copy, and notification_copy exist.

steps.name: ops_documentation
steps.instruction: Write deployment runbook (pre-deployment checklist, step-by-step procedure, rollback procedure, emergency contacts). Write incident response playbook (severity classification, escalation chain, common scenarios, post-incident review template). Write project README (description, badges, quick start, tech stack, contributing, license). Create CHANGELOG in Keep a Changelog format.
steps.done_when: deployment_runbook, incident_playbook, readme, and changelog exist.

steps.name: documentation_review_and_fixes
steps.instruction: Review all documentation for: technical accuracy, completeness, consistency, readability, formatting. Fix any issues found. Verify all links work, code examples are correct, and terminology is consistent.
steps.done_when: documentation_fixed exists confirming all review issues are resolved.

steps.name: spec_consistency_check_phase_12
steps.instruction: Z1 Tier 5B (B5) — wave-start spec_consistency_check at the head of phase 12. See 7.0z for full description.

steps.name: legal_documents
steps.instruction: Z6 T4A — render legal-doc drafts from compliance_overlay.required_documents[]. No LLM. Each writer-facing doc_type (tos / privacy_policy / cookie_banner) renders to mission_<id>/.compliance/legal/<artifact>.md via compliance_template_render. 12.1b (writer) then fills [LEGAL REVIEW REQUIRED] placeholders that can be filled from PRD + overlay context.
steps.done_when: terms_of_service, privacy_policy, and cookie_policy markdown files exist under mission_<id>/.compliance/legal/.

steps.name: legal_documents_fill
steps.instruction: Read the mechanical drafts at mission_<id>/.compliance/legal/{terms_of_service,privacy_policy,cookie_policy}.md and fill `[LEGAL REVIEW REQUIRED]` placeholders with project specifics drawn from the PRD final summary and compliance_overlay (service name, controller contact, data categories, retention period, declared lawful basis, listed sub-processors, etc.). Do NOT fill placeholders for items that require licensed counsel — keep `[LEGAL REVIEW REQUIRED]` markers on: jurisdiction-specific clauses (governing law, venue, dispute resolution, indemnification limits, liability caps), minimum-age threshold beyond what the overlay declares, WCAG conformance level, and any tax / regulator-specific obligations. Overwrite the three artifact files in place; do not move them.
steps.done_when: terms_of_service, privacy_policy, and cookie_policy reflect PRD/overlay specifics; [LEGAL REVIEW REQUIRED] markers remain only on counsel-required items.

steps.name: cookie_consent_implementation
steps.instruction: Implement a cookie consent banner and preference management system: consent banner on first visit, preference center with granular category control, logic to only set non-essential cookies after consent, consent state persistence, mechanism to withdraw consent.
steps.done_when: cookie_consent_implementation exists.

steps.name: compliance_checks
steps.instruction: Review for GDPR compliance: lawful basis, right to erasure, data portability, consent management, privacy by design, breach notification. Review for CCPA compliance: personal info categories disclosed, consumer rights documented, 'Do Not Sell' mechanism, verification procedures.
steps.done_when: gdpr_compliance_result and ccpa_compliance_result exist.

steps.name: license_audit_and_selection
steps.instruction: Audit all project dependencies for license compliance: identify license types, check compatibility, verify attribution requirements, flag copyleft licenses. Then recommend a project license based on audit results, business model, and contribution model. Trigger needs_clarification for human decision on license.
steps.done_when: license_audit_result and license_decision exist.

steps.name: legal_review
steps.instruction: Final legal review: internal consistency across all documents, no contradictions, all third-party services mentioned are actually used, all user rights are technically implementable, all placeholders flagged for professional legal review. Trigger needs_clarification for items requiring human/legal counsel.
steps.done_when: legal_review_result exists.

steps.name: production_infrastructure
steps.instruction: [NEEDS-REAL-TOOLS] Production provisioning requires real cloud-provider adapters (AWS/GCP/Azure/Vercel/Railway/Supabase/etc.) the agent does not currently have. Do NOT autonomously execute — surface a needs_clarification asking the human operator to provision and report back, then record their answer as the artifact. Provision all production infrastructure. Compute: instances or container orchestration, networking, load balancing, environment variables. Database: managed instance, automated backups, read replicas if needed, encryption at rest, connection pooling. Cache: managed cache service, appropriate memory allocation, encryption in transit, eviction policy.
steps.done_when: production_compute, production_database, and production_cache exist.

steps.name: network_and_security_infra
steps.instruction: Configure DNS (A/AAAA records, CNAME, MX, TXT for SPF/DKIM/DMARC). Set up SSL/TLS certificates with auto-renewal, HTTPS redirection, TLS 1.2 minimum, HSTS. Configure CDN for static assets with cache control. Set up WAF and DDoS protection with OWASP rule set.
steps.done_when: dns_configuration, ssl_configuration, cdn_configuration, and waf_configuration exist.

steps.name: network_and_security_infra_verify
steps.instruction: Z6 W3 — mechanical post-config verification: call cloudflare.list_zones() via vendor_call to confirm DNS provider is reachable with the credentials configured in 13.2. Failure emits founder_action(kind='generic') asking the founder to re-check the cloudflare API token. No LLM.
steps.done_when: vendor_call returns ok=true.

steps.name: monitoring_setup
steps.instruction: [NEEDS-REAL-TOOLS] Monitoring provisioning requires real APM/observability adapters (Datadog/Sentry/New Relic/Grafana Cloud/etc.) the agent does not currently have. Do NOT autonomously execute — surface a needs_clarification asking the human operator to wire up the monitoring stack and report back. Set up complete monitoring stack. APM: request tracing, distributed tracing, performance baselines. Infrastructure monitoring: CPU, memory, disk, network, database metrics, cache metrics. Error tracking: unhandled exceptions, source maps, alert thresholds. Log aggregation: structured log parsing, retention policies, saved searches. Uptime monitoring: synthetic checks every 1-5 minutes. Alert rules: error rate, response time degradation, resource exhaustion, certificate expiration. Build operational dashboard combining all metrics.
steps.done_when: All monitoring artifacts exist with verified functionality.

steps.name: monitoring_setup_verify
steps.instruction: Z6 W3 — mechanical post-config verification: call sentry.list_projects() via vendor_call to confirm the error-tracking provider is reachable with the credentials wired in 13.3. Failure emits founder_action(kind='generic'). No LLM.
steps.done_when: vendor_call returns ok=true.

steps.name: generate_incident_playbooks
steps.instruction: Z8 T4C — phase 13 playbook generator: from spec.architecture + spec.tech_stack, instantiate every matching incident_playbook_*_v1 recipe and emit the per-mission incident_playbooks artifact. The on-call agent (Z8 T4A) reads this artifact at alert time to know which playbooks are pre-blessed for this mission. No LLM.
steps.done_when: incident_playbooks artifact exists with a (possibly empty) playbook list.

steps.name: analytics_integration
steps.instruction: Instrument the product with standardized launch-time analytics so post-launch Growth digests have data. Use the analytics_instrumentation/v1 recipe (PostHog). Steps: (1) pick_recipe / instantiate_recipe analytics_instrumentation v1 - this writes the posthog-js client shim (track_event helper for web), the posthog-python server shim (track_event for backend), and the events module carrying the standard AARRR event taxonomy. (2) Read the success_metrics artifact from step 2.9; for EACH entry in success_metrics.aarrr_metrics, determine its AARRR funnel stage and map it to the matching standard event name(s): acquisition -> landing_view/signup_started/signup_completed; activation -> first_value_event (rename per product); retention -> session_started (attach day_of_cohort); revenue -> checkout_started/checkout_completed/subscription_created/subscription_cancelled; referral -> share_initiated/share_completed/invite_redeemed. (3) Insert track_event(name, properties) calls at the code sites that realize each metric's formula - web shim for browser-observable events, server shim for webhook-confirmed events (checkout_completed, subscription_*, signup_completed). (4) Wire initAnalytics() at app bootstrap and setAnalyticsContext() with mission_id/feature_id/business_model; every event auto-attaches mission_id, feature_id, variant, segment, business_model. (5) PostHog init reads POSTHOG_API_KEY / POSTHOG_HOST (NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefixed on the web side). (6) Configure conversion funnels, retention cohorts and a KPI dashboard tied to success_metrics.north_star_metric. Respect cookie consent. Record every instrumented event name and every file created/edited in the analytics_instrumentation artifact.
steps.done_when: analytics_integration, event_tracking, analytics_dashboards and analytics_instrumentation exist; analytics_instrumentation lists the instrumented event names and the shim files created, with at least one event mapped per success_metrics.aarrr_metrics entry.

steps.name: email_and_feature_flags
steps.instruction: Set up email delivery: transactional email service, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, email templates, bounce handling, suppression lists. Configure feature flags for production: create flags for each major feature, percentage rollout capability, user segment targeting.
steps.done_when: email_delivery_config and feature_flags_production exist.

steps.name: production_data_setup
steps.instruction: Run database migrations against production database. Verify each migration completes without errors. Seed production with required initial data: system config, default roles/permissions, admin user (credentials in secrets manager), lookup tables. Do NOT seed test data.
steps.done_when: production_migration_result and production_seed_result exist.

steps.name: autoscaling_and_rate_limiting
steps.instruction: Configure autoscaling: horizontal policies based on CPU/memory/request count, min/max instances, cooldown periods. Configure rate limiting for all API endpoints: global per-IP, per-user for authenticated, stricter for sensitive endpoints, appropriate response headers and 429 responses.
steps.done_when: autoscaling_config and rate_limit_config exist.

steps.name: pre_launch_verification
steps.instruction: Run full regression test suite against staging with production-like configuration and feature flags. Run performance tests against production infrastructure and compare against NFR targets. Verify all critical user flows work end-to-end.
steps.done_when: final_regression_results and production_performance_results exist.

steps.name: production_security_and_email_verification
steps.instruction: Run final security scan against production: SSL/TLS score, security headers, WAF rules active, no debug endpoints exposed. Test email deliverability: send test emails to Gmail/Outlook/Yahoo, verify inbox delivery, SPF/DKIM/DMARC authentication, HTML rendering, bounce handling.
steps.done_when: production_security_scan and email_deliverability_results exist.

steps.name: seo_implementation
steps.instruction: Implement SEO: unique title tags and meta descriptions, Open Graph and Twitter Card tags, structured data (JSON-LD), canonical URLs, XML sitemap, robots.txt, semantic HTML5, descriptive alt text.
steps.done_when: seo_implementation exists.

steps.name: social_preview_test
steps.instruction: Mechanical: fetch each URL listed in seo_implementation and verify the served HTML exposes Open Graph + Twitter Card meta tags, plus that og:image is reachable. This is a necessary but not sufficient gate for social rendering — Facebook/Twitter/LinkedIn debuggers may still reject (size/length/UA quirks). Pass means every required tag is present and og:image HEAD returns 2xx for every URL.
steps.done_when: social_preview_results.pass_fail == 'pass'.

steps.name: stripe_provision_products
steps.instruction: Z6 T5B — drive vendor_call(stripe, create_product/create_price) for every priced item in monetization_strategy.products[]. Idempotent via metadata.kutay_id (re-runs detect existing products and skip). Output artifact stripe_provisioned is consumed by 13.12 payment_flow_test.
steps.done_when: stripe_provisioned exists with one item per priced product (or skipped when no Stripe billing).

steps.name: payment_flow_test
steps.instruction: Z6 T5C — exercise the real Stripe test API end-to-end via vendor_call: create test customer → checkout session for first provisioned price → confirm with test card 4242... → list_subscriptions → cancel → retrieve_balance. All vendor_call() — no direct HTTP. On dispute-related failure emit founder_action(kind='legal_counsel'); on generic failure emit kind='generic'.
steps.done_when: payment_flow_results exists with each step's pass/fail + stripe IDs + timestamps.

steps.name: backup_and_rollback_verification
steps.instruction: Verify backup: trigger manual backup, restore to test instance, verify data integrity, measure restore time vs RTO. Verify rollback: deploy current version to staging, deploy simulated broken version, execute rollback, verify application functional after rollback, measure rollback time.
steps.done_when: backup_verification and rollback_verification exist.

steps.name: launch_go_no_go
steps.instruction: Compile all pre-launch verification results into a go/no-go assessment. Review: all tests passing, all SLAs met, no critical vulnerabilities, emails reaching inbox, recovery works, rollback verified. Create a go/no-go checklist. Trigger needs_clarification for final human launch approval.
steps.done_when: launch_go_no_go exists with human approval.

steps.name: launch_approval_green
steps.instruction: Z10 T1A/T3C milestone commit: launch_go_no_go approved => atomic push + mark_green snapshot. Pre-launch all-tests-pass checkpoint (rollback target if launch fails).
steps.done_when: Workspace committed (if changes) + remote push succeeded + mission_green_tags row appended.

steps.name: launch_planning
steps.instruction: Create detailed launch checklist with time-sequenced steps: pre-launch (backup, maintenance mode, deploy, migrations), during-launch (smoke test, monitor errors, verify integrations), post-launch (remove feature flags, enable analytics, announcements). Determine optimal launch timing considering timezone, day of week, team availability. Trigger needs_clarification to confirm launch date/time.
steps.done_when: launch_checklist and launch_timing exist with confirmed schedule.

steps.name: launch_checklist_review
steps.instruction: Review launch_checklist: all steps have success criteria, rollback triggers defined, timing realistic, dependencies correct, communication plan included.
steps.done_when: checklist_review exists.

steps.name: execute_launch
steps.instruction: Switch payment system from sandbox to live (if applicable). Execute production launch following launch_checklist: trigger deployment pipeline, run migrations, verify health checks, execute smoke tests, confirm all integrations functional. Monitor error rates and response times for first 30 minutes.
steps.done_when: launch_enabled exists confirming the application is live and stable.

steps.name: launch_monitoring_and_stability
steps.instruction: Monitor the launched application intensively during first critical hours. Watch: error rates, response times, infrastructure utilization, user signup rates, alerts triggered. Log all observations. Then assess stability: classify as green/yellow/red. Trigger needs_clarification if yellow or red.
steps.done_when: launch_monitoring_log and stability_assessment exist.

steps.name: marketing_site_publish
steps.instruction: Deploy marketing/landing site to production. Verify all links point to live application, signup flow works end-to-end, analytics tracking active, social sharing previews work.
steps.done_when: marketing_site_live exists.

steps.name: launch_announcements
steps.instruction: Draft Product Hunt launch post: tagline (<60 chars), description (<260 chars), first comment with product story. Draft social media posts for Twitter/X, LinkedIn with platform-appropriate copy, hashtags, and follow-up engagement posts.
steps.done_when: product_hunt_launch and social_launch_posts exist.

steps.name: launch_email
steps.instruction: Draft launch email: compelling subject line (and A/B variant), preview text, body with product intro and key value props, CTA button, unsubscribe link. Format as HTML email template.
steps.done_when: launch_email exists.

steps.name: app_store_submission
steps.instruction: Prepare and submit app store listing materials for iOS App Store and/or Google Play: title, subtitle, description, keywords, screenshots, privacy policy URL, data safety questionnaire. Trigger needs_clarification for developer account credentials.
steps.done_when: app_store_submission exists.

steps.name: launch_retrospective
steps.instruction: Conduct launch retrospective: what went well, what didn't, initial user metrics, time from launch to first signup, incidents or near-misses, accuracy of pre-launch testing, effectiveness of monitoring. Document lessons learned.
steps.done_when: launch_retrospective exists.

steps.name: arm_analytics_digest_cron
steps.instruction: Z9 T2B mechanical post-launch step (no LLM): arm a weekly analytics_digest cron for this mission. Idempotent — writes a durable {action, interval_seconds} entry into missions.cursor[cron] (restart-replayed by arm_from_cursor) and live-arms via mission_cron.arm(). Re-running never double-arms.
steps.done_when: analytics_digest cron armed for the mission.

steps.name: post_launch_monitoring
steps.instruction: Monitor production error tracking and performance continuously during first week. For errors: categorize by severity, identify patterns, track error rate trends, escalate P0/P1 immediately. For performance: compare against NFR targets, check for degradation trends, memory leaks, resource utilization forecasts.
steps.done_when: error_monitoring_log and performance_report exist.
steps.trigger: Continuous during first week, then triggered by alerts

steps.name: feedback_system
steps.instruction: Implement user feedback collection AND wire external signal intake. (1) In-app: feedback widget, feedback API endpoint with context, NPS/satisfaction survey at key milestones, feedback admin view for review and categorization. (2) Signal intake: register the deployed product's webhook endpoints with the support and analytics vendors so external signals flow back into KutAI — Intercom (support conversations) and Zendesk (support tickets) via their webhook/subscription APIs, and PostHog (analytics events) via its action/webhook hooks. Point each vendor at the KutAI webhook listener path POST /webhook/{provider} (provider in intercom|zendesk|posthog). Store the per-provider webhook signing secret in the integration config (webhook_secret env-ref) and record the integration_mappings row binding this product's integration_id to its mission so inbound signals route correctly. All inbound signals are HMAC-verified, PII-redacted, and stored as raw_signal growth_events for later classification and prioritization.
steps.done_when: feedback_system exists and intercom/zendesk/posthog webhooks are registered against the product's signal-intake endpoints.

steps.name: daily_health_report
steps.instruction: Generate daily health report: uptime, error rate, response time p95, active users, new signups, key business metrics, infrastructure utilization, incidents/alerts.
steps.done_when: daily_health_report exists.

steps.name: incident_response
steps.instruction: Respond to monitoring alerts following incident_playbook: classify severity, investigate root cause, apply fix, verify resolution, document incident with timeline, root cause, impact, resolution, and preventive measures.
steps.done_when: incident_report exists.

steps.name: weekly_analytics_and_feedback
steps.instruction: Review product analytics: acquisition, engagement (DAU/WAU/MAU), retention (D1/D7/D30), conversion funnels, business metrics. Compare against targets. Analyze user feedback: categorize as bug reports, feature requests, usability complaints, positive feedback. Identify patterns, themes, and trending topics.
steps.done_when: weekly_analytics and feedback_analysis_report exist.

steps.name: weekly_summary
steps.instruction: Write weekly summary: key metric highlights with week-over-week comparisons, notable feedback themes, bugs fixed and outstanding, infrastructure health, upcoming work, decisions needed. Format for stakeholder consumption. Trigger needs_clarification if decisions need human input.
steps.done_when: weekly_summary_report exists.

steps.name: bug_triage_and_fixing
steps.instruction: Run the Z9 growth signal-to-backlog loop for bugs. (1) Classify: the mechanical 'classify_signals' executor fetches raw_signal growth_events and enqueues the signal_classifier agent via Beckman — each signal gets a label (bug/feature_request/churn_signal/pricing_feedback/praise/spam) and a recipe domain. (2) Score: the mechanical 'score_backlog' executor scores classified bug-labelled signals (frequency × revenue_impact × north_star_relevance × age_decay / cost_band_weight) and writes top-N backlog_candidate rows. (3) Triage the bug candidates from /backlog: assign severity (P0-P3), determine affected user count, identify reproduction steps, assign to sprint. P0 bugs flagged for immediate hotfix. (4) Fix bugs in priority order — only candidates the founder /approve-d become missions; reproduce, identify root cause, implement fix, write regression tests, verify.
steps.done_when: bug_triage_report and bug_fix_log exist.

steps.name: feature_request_prioritization
steps.instruction: Prioritize feature requests via the Z9 growth signal-to-backlog loop. (1) Classify: the mechanical 'classify_signals' executor enqueues the signal_classifier agent through Beckman, labelling raw_signal growth_events — feature_request signals are mapped to a recipe domain. (2) Score: the mechanical 'score_backlog' executor ranks feature_request clusters with score = frequency × revenue_impact × north_star_relevance × age_decay / cost_band_weight, writing top-N backlog_candidate rows with an inspectable formula breakdown. (3) Surface: the founder reviews the ranked candidates via the /backlog command; /approve <id> is the ONLY path that spawns a mission — there is no auto-spawn. Produce a ranked feature_request_backlog with the score breakdown and recommended sprint for each candidate.
steps.done_when: feature_request_backlog exists.

steps.name: maintenance_operations
steps.instruction: Run dependency updates: identify outdated deps, apply patch updates, evaluate minor updates, flag major for review, run tests after each batch, scan for new vulnerabilities. Perform database maintenance: analyze/vacuum, review slow queries, check bloat, verify backup integrity, review storage growth.
steps.done_when: dependency_update_log and database_maintenance_log exist.

steps.name: infrastructure_cost_and_security
steps.instruction: Review infrastructure costs: compute, database, network, CDN, monitoring. Compare against budget. Identify optimization opportunities. Trigger needs_clarification if cost exceeds thresholds. Review security advisories: check for new CVEs, framework announcements, certificate expiration. Apply critical patches immediately, queue non-critical for next sprint.
steps.done_when: infrastructure_cost_report and security_patch_log exist.

steps.name: record_demo
steps.instruction: Z10 T4A + z10-wire-fixes F3: record an end-of-mission Playwright e2e demo, trim to <=90s, save as data/missions/{id}/demo.mp4. scenario_path resolution order: payload.scenario_path override > missions.demo_scenario_path > newest tests/e2e/*.spec.ts > no_e2e_specs skip path. Runs inside per-mission Docker container.
steps.done_when: demo.mp4 exists and is a real mp4 video.

steps.name: verify_demo_artifact
steps.instruction: Z10 T4A: gate-check demo.mp4 — must exist, be video/mp4, >1MB, >5s.
steps.done_when: verify_demo_artifact reports ok=True.

steps.name: new_feature_cycle
steps.instruction: Close the hypothesis verdict loop before opening the next iteration. First, review verdicts: read the mission's hypotheses (predicted metric impact + recorded verdict) and mission_lessons rows with source_kind='hypothesis_verdict'. Confirmed verdicts validated the bet; refuted verdicts are suppressed for 90 days; inconclusive verdicts need tighter measurement. Summarize what the last iteration's predictions actually delivered. The founder reviews these via the Telegram /hypothesis command; the next-iteration feature is founder-gated (the /approve pattern) and is NOT auto-spawned. Then, for the founder-approved highest-priority item only: write feature specification (problem statement, target persona, proposed solution, acceptance criteria, out-of-scope, technical considerations); create UX/UI design building on existing design_handoff patterns; implement database migrations, API endpoints, frontend components, tests, documentation updates. Feature should be behind a feature flag, and the spec must record a predicted metric impact (metric, direction, magnitude) so a new hypothesis can be recorded for the next verdict cycle.
steps.done_when: Prior-iteration hypothesis verdicts and hypothesis_verdict lessons have been reviewed and summarized; and feature_specification, feature_design, and feature_implementation exist for the founder-approved next item.

steps.name: release_cycle
steps.instruction: Plan release: version number (semver), included changes, timeline, rollout strategy, rollback criteria. Write release notes (technical changelog + user-facing announcement). Execute deployment following release_plan and cd_production_config. Monitor for 24-48 hours post-release.
steps.done_when: release_plan, release_notes, and release_deployment_log exist.

steps.name: technical_debt_tracking
steps.instruction: Track technical debt AND run the feature-lifecycle (sunset) loop. First, the debt register: TODO/FIXME/HACK comments, deprecated API usage, outdated patterns, test coverage gaps, architecture limitations, implementation shortcuts — score each by risk and effort and produce a prioritized list for sprint planning. Second, review the feature-sunset queue: the weekly mechanical `sunset_score_recompute` cron scores every shipped feature by usage (distinct-user reach over the last 30d, from growth_events + recipe_pin_log) against maintenance cost (cost_band), and writes `sunset_candidate` growth_events for features whose usage is below the founder-set threshold (default <1% of active users) yet still cost money to maintain. The founder reviews these via the Telegram /sunset command; a feature deprecation is founder-gated (the /approve_sunset pattern, mirroring /approve) and is NEVER auto-executed — only the founder spawns the one deprecation mission. Summarize which features are sunset candidates and why (usage_rate, cost_band, the inspectable breakdown), and fold confirmed-low-value features into the technical_debt_register as deprecation candidates so they enter sprint planning alongside debt items.
steps.done_when: technical_debt_register exists; sunset candidates have been reviewed and any founder-approved deprecations are reflected in the register.

steps.name: roadmap_update
steps.instruction: Update product roadmap AND run the north-star sync. First, north-star sync: the weekly mechanical `roadmap_northstar_sync` cron reads the success_metrics artifact (step 2.9) and checks the declared north_star_metric against recent reality — it writes a `northstar_review` growth_events row when the metric is undefined (never set), untracked (declared but no growth_events measure it in the last 30d), or flat (its readings have not moved across the window). Review any open northstar_review rows: a stale north-star means the roadmap is steering by an outdated or unmeasured compass. If the north-star is stale, refine it — pick a metric that is both tracked and still has room to grow, and update success_metrics accordingly. Second, update the product roadmap: reassess feature priorities based on user data and feedback, adjust capacity based on velocity, update strategic direction (anchored on the now-current north-star), plan technical investments, project timelines. Trigger needs_clarification for human approval.
steps.done_when: quarterly_roadmap exists with human approval; any stale north-star flagged by northstar_review has been reviewed and refined.

steps.name: mission_deliverable_bundle
steps.instruction: Z10 T4A: post end-of-mission deliverable bundle (demo.mp4 + commit + provenance + cost) to the mission's Telegram thread.
steps.done_when: deliverable bundle posted to Telegram.

steps.name: component_library_decision
steps.instruction:
Z1 Tier 2 (C7) — pick the component library for the frontend stack chosen in 4.2. Read the product_charter (Solutions We Own + Brand Keywords) and the tech_stack_decision ADR. Evaluate at least 3 candidates appropriate to the stack (e.g. for React: shadcn/ui, MUI, Mantine, Chakra, Ant Design, or custom). For each option include rationale_for / rationale_against / tech_maturity_score / novelty_benefit / reversal_cost / monthly_cost_curve (most are free OSS — express commercial-license gotchas in the cost field).

Emit as a universal-shape ADR JSON at `mission_{mission_id}/.adr/component_library_decision.json` AND append the row to `mission_{mission_id}/.adr/register.md`. The ADR MUST include a `stack_adr_ref` field pointing at the tech_stack_decision ADR's `adr_id` so the reviewer can confirm the linkage. All universal-shape rules from steps 4.1/4.2 apply (see verify_adr_shape mechanical check). Phase 5 design steps will read this ADR by id.

Z1 Tier 2 (P3+C7+A8): emit your decision as a universal-shape ADR JSON file at `mission_{mission_id}/.adr/<adr_id>.json` AND append a row to `mission_{mission_id}/.adr/register.md` (one line per ADR: `- ADR-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN — <title> — status:<status>`). The ADR JSON MUST contain ALL of these top-level fields:
- `_schema_version`: "1"
- `adr_id`: e.g. `ADR-{date}-{seq3}` (date = today YYYY-MM-DD, seq3 = zero-padded 3-digit sequence per mission)
- `title`: one-line decision name
- `status`: one of `proposed | accepted | deprecated | superseded`
- `context`: problem/situation paragraph
- `decision`: chosen option, prose
- `consequences`: trade-offs incurred (positive + negative)
- `options_considered`: list of >=2 options. EACH option has `id` (e.g. OPT-A), `name`, `rationale_for`, `rationale_against`, `tech_maturity_score` (0-10 heuristic), `novelty_benefit` (str), `reversal_cost` (low|medium|high)
- `chosen_option_id`: MUST resolve to one of the option ids above
- `falsification_signal`: when would we revisit this? measurable (e.g. "if active SKU > 200 by week 4")
- `reversal_cost`: low|medium|high (overall ADR-level)
- `supersedes_adr_id`: null or a prior `ADR-…` id

The mechanical post-hook `verify_adr_shape` will reject the ADR if any field is missing, `chosen_option_id` is orphan, `falsification_signal` is empty, or `reversal_cost` is outside the allowed enum.
steps.done_when: component_library_decision.json exists with universal ADR shape + stack_adr_ref + cost_curve per option.

steps.name: architecture_pattern_decision_shape_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical post-check for step 4.1 (architecture_pattern): asserts the universal-shape ADR fields (P3) are present + well-formed. Failure forces the LLM step to regenerate.
steps.done_when: verify_adr_shape returns ok=true.

steps.name: tech_stack_decision_shape_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical post-check for step 4.2 (tech_stack): asserts the universal-shape ADR fields (P3) are present + well-formed. Failure forces the LLM step to regenerate.
steps.done_when: verify_adr_shape returns ok=true.

steps.name: tech_stack_decision_cost_curve_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical post-check (A8) for stack step 4.2: every option in the ADR has a populated monthly_cost_curve and the ADR carries cost_at_target_users_usd + cost_mitigation_plan.
steps.done_when: verify_cost_curve_present returns ok=true.

steps.name: database_schema_decision_shape_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical post-check for step 4.4 (database): asserts the universal-shape ADR fields (P3) are present + well-formed. Failure forces the LLM step to regenerate.
steps.done_when: verify_adr_shape returns ok=true.

steps.name: database_schema_decision_cost_curve_check
steps.instruction: Mechanical post-check (A8) for stack step 4.4: every option in the ADR has a populated monthly_cost_curve and the ADR carries cost_at_target_users_usd + cost_mitigation_plan.
steps.done_when: verify_cost_curve_present returns ok=true.

steps.name: auth_design_decision_shape_check
